name: Center for Public Service
description: We invite you to explore the variety of programs and resources available at the Center for Public Service.  Whether you are interested in finding opportunities to complete your public service graduation requirement, developing your leadership skills through service or getting involved in extracurricular service activities, the Center for Public Service staff is here to assist you. Through our various student programs and resources, we are here to support your personal service commitments and academic interests.
summary: The Center supports a University curriculum and research agenda by uniting academics and action, classroom and communities through which students, faculty, and community partners dedicate themselves to the transformation of civic life.

name: Quiz Bowl
description:
We are a club who meets twice a week, Tuesdays and Fridays, at 7:00 PM in BOGGs 102 to play Quiz Bowl, a trivia game with a variety of subjects. Topics range from science to history to literature to pop culture (and everything in between!). We also attend tournaments all over the Gulf South. Join the Group Me to stay up to date with practice dates and times. 

Group Me: https://groupme.com/join_group/43947128/LS986n 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: We are a club that meets twice weekly to play Quiz Bowl, a trivia game with a variety of subjects. Topics range from science to history to literature to pop culture (and everything in between!). We also attend tournaments all over the country.

name: Shockwave Dance Team
description:
Shockwave Dance Team is the official dance team of Tulane University. We perform at home football and basketball games and we march in Mardi Gras parades. We form an integral part of the Tulane University Marching Band. We also compete at NDA nationals!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Shockwave Dance Team is the official dance team of Tulane University. We perform at home football and basketball games and we march in Mardi Gras parades. We form an integral part of the Tulane University Marching Band. We also compete at NDA nationals!

name: Tulane School of Architecture Student Government
description:
Student government organization of the Tulane University School of Architecture and Built Environment students. Focused on engaging students in all TuSA Programs and the community in a social and professional capacity through outreach and engagement

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Student government organization of the Tulane University School of Architecture students. Focused on engaging students in all TuSA Programs and the community in a social and professional capacity through outreach and engagement.

name: Fencing Club
description: Welcome to the Tulane Fencing Club! We meet Tuesdays and Thursdays from 8-10pm on the basketball courts in the back of Reily. Like to fence? Come join us! Everyone is welcome to come as often as they would like. Beginning lessons are also offered from the beginning of the semester for those with little to no experience. Any questions? Contact Indigo Ward at nward@tulane.edu.
summary: Welcome to the Tulane Fencing Club. We meet Tuesdays and Thursdays from 8-10pm on the basketball courts in the back of Reily (Uptown Gym). Like to fence? Come join us! Everyone is welcome to come as often as they would like.

name: Classics Club
description:
The Tulane Classics Club is devoted to the promotion of Classics and the love of classical antiquity, not only of Rome and Greece, but also the Near East, Egypt, Britannia, and everywhere in between. We advance an appreciation of Classics and Mediterranean antiquity through film screenings, lectures, meetings, interactive games and activities, and service learning opportunities. We are a social and academic organization, where talking and asking questions further our goals.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Tulane Classics Club is devoted to the promotion of Classics and the love of classical antiquity, not only of Rome and Greece, but also the Near East, Egypt, Britannia, and everywhere in between.

name: Rugby Club - Men's
description: This is the official OrgSync page for Tulane Men's Rugby. As there is no collegiate varsity rugby, club is the highest level of competition. We make no cuts, anyone who comes out to practice is welcome on the team. We compete in our conference in the fall semester (15s), and schedule non-conference matches and Rugby 7s tournaments in the spring. No experience is necessary. In the fall of 2016 we were runners up at the 38th Annual Battleship Invitational and finished the fall season with a 7-1 record. In the Fall of 2015 we were the runners up at the 37th Annual Battleship Invitational. In the Spring of 2016 we qualified to go to the USA Rugby 7s National Championships. In the falls 2012 and 2014 were the champions of the Battleship Rugby Tournament. In Spring of 2019, we were the champions of the Louisiana Colligate Rugby Tournament. We have made appearances at the Collegiate Rugby National 7s Championship in both 2020 and 2021. In the Fall 2022 and 2023 season we were the Deep South Conference Runner Up. In the Spring 2023 season we placed top 3 at two national tournaments.
summary: Tulane Men's Rugby. All players of any experience level are welcome.

name: Queer Student Alliance
description:
Tulane's Queer Student Alliance is the oldest continuously-operating Gay and Lesbian organization in the state of Louisiana. 2025-2026 marks the 53rd year that we've been out, loud and proud on campus. We are an alliance that embraces the similarities and differences of all people, whether identifying as LGBTQIA or as an ally. As a group, we seek to promote awareness, equality, and compassion in order to cultivate a healthy environment for all individuals at Tulane. Our goal is to preserve queer culture through open discussion and progressive programming. We also wish to promote a safe space where like-minded individuals can foster lasting friendships.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Tulane's Queer Student Alliance is the oldest continuously-operating Gay and Lesbian organization in the state of Louisiana. 2024-2025 marks the 52nd year that we've been out, loud and proud on campus. We are an alliance that embraces the similarities...

organizationType.name: Recognized Student Organizations - Multicultural, Religious & GSAC

name: Rugby Club - Women's
description:
Tulane Women's Rugby Club is a great way to get involved, stay in shape, and feel empowered! Our team plays in both competitive and friendly matches all across the south. Join one of the only women's collegiate teams in Louisiana and become part of something great!

In the past few years, we have qualified for the Division II Spring National Championships. In 2016 and 2018 we were the Division II Spring National Champions. In 2017 we were the runner-ups. We look forward to adding new players and constantly improving ourselves on and off the field. The club is a large time commitment, but the most empowering experience! There is no experience needed to join! We will take the time to teach the rookies everything they need to know. We love new players and enjoy spreading the love of the sport.
summary: Tulane Women's Rugby Club plays competitive and friendly matches across the south. Join one of the only women's collegiate teams in Louisiana and become part of something great! There is no experience needed to join!

name: Community Action Council of Tulane University Students
description:
CACTUS has sponsored community service and social awareness programming at Tulane since 1968. This council supports all student-led community service activities and helps to cultivate relationships and initiatives that meet the needs of our community partners and student volunteers. Our purpose is to serve as the umbrella organization that supports Tulane University-sponsored clubs with a community service focus and to guide and support individual service initiatives. Join our service opportunities GroupMe here. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: We have been sponsoring community service and social awareness programming at Tulane since 1968. This council supports all student-led community service activities and helps to cultivate relationships and initiatives that meet the needs of our community.

name: Literary Society
description:
The Tulane Literary Society aims to build a community of English and Art enthusiasts to inspire each other to read, write, and cultivate skills as both writers and readers. Through our work putting together one review per semester, we aim to create a supporting space to discuss the works of both professionals and peers, while simultaneously honing our skills as scholars. Our other goal is to remain a semi-consistent presence in our club members' lives to inspire creation, to remind to create, and look for the opportunity to create. We want to raise awareness, positivity, and drive for reading and writing.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Tulane Literary Society publishes “The Tulane Review,” which showcases works of poetry, prose and art from across the globe. We aim to build a community of art and literature enthusiasts to inspire each other.

name: Water Polo Club
description: Welcome to the Tulane Club Water Polo, where the thrill of aquatic competition meets the camaraderie of team spirit. Our club provides a dynamic platform for Tulane University students to dive into the exciting world of water polo, blending athleticism, strategy, and sheer enthusiasm. Whether you're a seasoned player or new to the sport, our inclusive community offers opportunities for skill development, friendly scrimmages, and spirited tournaments.
summary: Tulane Water Polo invites everyone to join us! We practice Tuesdays & Thursdays from 6:30-8 in the pool at the Reily Center. Water Polo experience is not required, you just need to know how to swim!

name: Chi Alpha
description:
Chi Alpha Christian Fellowship

We are about faith, community and action: We are a community of friends putting our deepest values into action together. We do this within the context of a sincere Christian faith, while making space for seekers and friends from other traditions or non-religious perspectives to join us in a life worth living.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: We are about faith, community and action: We do this within the context of a sincere Christian faith, while making space for seekers and friends from other traditions or non-religious perspectives to join us in a life worth living.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students
description:
Bridging between the academic and professional architecture communities, the American Institute of Architecture Students at Tulane is our campus chapter of the official organization of our profession, the American Institute of Architects. We bring the profession to you through on-campus programming and bring you to the profession through networking events and firm visits.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Bridging between the academic and professional architecture communities, the American Institute of Architecture Students at Tulane is our campus chapter of the official organization of our profession, the American Institute of Architects.

name: Alpha Rho Chi
description:
Alpha Rho Chi (APX) is the national fraternity for architecture and the allied arts. Our organization unites men and women for the purpose of fellowship and lifelong friendships and our mutual interest in professional development. This is exemplified by the fraternity’s motto: Fidelitas, Amor et Artes or “Fidelity and Love of the Arts.”

Our student and alumni members study and practice architecture, interior design, architectural engineering, landscape architecture, and related disciplines. Alpha Rho Chi is a charter member of the Professional Fraternity Association.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Alpha Rho Chi (APX) is the national, co-educational professional fraternity for architecture and the allied arts.

name: Tower and Crescent
description:
As students of Tulane University, we recognize the important role that alumni play in the life of the University. As Tower and Crescent Members, we strive to develop a strong link between alumni and current students through philanthropy and engagement. We provide opportunities for students to network with the Tulanians that have gone before you, linking the past with the future of Tulane.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: T&C is the student-alumni organization at Tulane! We host events to build Tulane spirit; we attend alumni events and serve as ambassadors for Tulane, and we create and steward Tulane traditions.

name: Society of Women Engineers
description:
For more than seven decades, SWE has given women engineers a unique place and voice within the engineering industry. Our organization is centered around a passion for our members' success and continues to evolve with the challenges and opportunities reflected in today's exciting engineering and technology specialties. We invite you to explore the values, principles, and priorities that guide our initiatives and learn how together, SWE can continue to make a lasting impact on the future.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: For more than 7 decades, Society of Women Engineers (SWE) has given women engineers a unique place and voice within the engineering industry. Our organization is centered around a passion for our members' success within engineering and tech specialties.

name: Tulane Undergraduate Assembly
description:
We are the Tulane Undergraduate Assembly, our purpose is to act as the advocating body of the undergraduate students of Tulane University.


We are empowered to listen, understand, and advocate for the will of the student body. We serve as advocates for students, communicate students’ views and ideas, serve as liaisons between students and Tulane University administration, improve the overall student experience, and serve as points of information and will do so in order to afford all undergraduate students the opportunity to participate in the management of their own affairs.


We acknowledge and pay tribute to the original inhabitants of this land, including the Choctaw, Houma, Chitimacha, Biloxi, and other Native peoples. The city of New Orleans is a continuation of an indigenous trade hub on the Mississippi River, known for thousands of years as Bulbancha. Native peoples have lived on this land since time immemorial, and the resilient voices of Native Americans remain an inseparable part of our local culture. With gratitude and honor, we acknowledge the indigenous nations that have lived and continue to thrive here.


As advocates who center the undergraduate student experience in the decision-making process of the university; we will amplify and be accountable to the voices of the historically unheard and underrepresented. We welcome everyone to bring their own perspective and experience to advance our mission. In alignment with Tulane’s Office of Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion, we commit to learn, practice, and lead with equity, justice, and inclusion to create an anti-racist community where all students can grow and thrive.

Learn more at tua.tulane.edu

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The purpose of the Tulane Undergraduate Assembly is to act as the advocating body of the undergraduate students of Tulane University.

name: Lavin-Bernick Center for University Life & the Commons
description: The LBC & Commons is your hub for getting involved, hosting events, dining, shopping, building community, and more!
summary: The LBC & Commons is your hub for getting involved, hosting events, dining, shopping, building community, and more!

name: NAMI On-Campus
description:
NAMI, the National Alliance on Mental Illness, is the nation's largest grassroots mental health organization dedicated to building better lives for the millions of Americans affected by mental illness. NAMI advocates for access to services, treatment, supports and research and is steadfast in its commitment to raising awareness and building a community of hope for all those in need.

NAMI on Campus clubs are student-led clubs that tackle mental health issues on campus by raising mental health awareness, educating the campus community, supporting students, promoting services and supports and advocating. These clubs are open to all students, whether they have a mental health condition, are a family member or friend or have an interest in mental health. NAMI on Campus clubs aim to address the mental health needs of all students so they have positive, successful and rewarding college experiences.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) on Campus: Tulane is dedicated to ending the stigma surrounding mental illness, promoting mental health, and educating the campus community about available resources.

name: Newcomb-Tulane College Academic Enrichment
description: The Newcomb-Tulane College Office of Academic Enrichment works to connect students with faculty, research and personal development opportunities, and intellectual community. Connect with our Office of Undergraduate Research to discover research opportunities in a variety of disciplines. Apply for a grant to fund your research, internship, or special project. Experience more in your sophomore year by becoming a College Scholar and diving into Sophomore September. Complete a Senior Thesis to pursue your own original research or creative work under the guidance of Tulane's world-class faculty.
summary: The Newcomb-Tulane College Office of Academic Enrichment works to connect students with faculty, research and personal development opportunities, and intellectual community.

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
description: The Alpha Phi Chapter of Kappa Alpha Theta is a Greek organization on Tulane's campus. Kappa Alpha Theta Fraternity, founded at Indiana Asbury College (now DePauw University) in Greencastle, Indiana, on January 27, 1870, is the first Greek-letter fraternity for women. “Kappa Alpha Theta,” “Theta,” and our coat of arms, badges, and logo are registered trademarks of Kappa Alpha Theta Fraternity.
summary: Kappa Alpha Theta is a Greek organization on Tulane’s campus.

name: Ultimate Frisbee Club - Women's
description:
We are the Tulane Women's Club Ultimate Frisbee Team. We practice 3 times a week on Tuesday/Thursday night and Sunday morning. Every semester we travel to competitive tournaments throughout the Gulf South at different colleges such as LSU, FSU, and Vanderbilt. Our team welcomes those with no frisbee experience as well as those who have played before.

Feel free to reach out to any member of our eboard with questions!
summary: We are the Tulane Women's Club Ultimate Frisbee Team. Each semester we travel to competitive tournaments throughout the Gulf South, at different colleges such as FSU, Auburn, and Vanderbilt. We practice 3 times a week on Tuesday/Thursday night and Sunday

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description:
Tulane’s chapter of Alpha Phi Omega is a coeducational community service fraternity dedicated to developing leadership, fostering friendship, and promoting service on campus and in the New Orleans community. Rooted in the values of Leadership, Friendship, and Service, our chapter brings together students who are passionate about making a positive impact. We welcome new members through recruitment each semester and are always excited to grow our community of compassionate, driven individuals.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: APO is a national co-ed service fraternity engaging in community service throughout New Orleans. We are guided by the principles of Leadership, Friendship, and Service. We partner with a variety of local community organizations to prepare service events.

name: Newcomb Institute
description:
Newcomb Institute is a place where all students interested in pursuing scholarship and training for gender equity can flourish. We connect students with faculty, staff, and other students who share similar interests who can guide them. Consistent with Tulane University’s core mission and values, the faculty and staff at the Newcomb Institute are committed to education, equity, collaboration, diversity, creativity, and stewardship. 

To learn more about Newcomb and their contributions to Tulane and the community, visit newcomb.tulane.edu
summary: Newcomb Institute shapes the future by educating students to achieve gender equity.

Established in 2017, Cookies & Code is Tulane's premier technology club dedicated to organizing events related to computer science. Please visit our website: https://cookiesandcode.net

description: The mission of the Office of International Students and Scholars is to provide leadership in the internationalization of Tulane University by supporting the Tulane international community and facilitating cross-cultural interaction. By way of support, OISS offers quality programs and services and ensures compliance with federal immigration regulations.
summary: OISS assists Tulane’s international community with immigration, cultural adjustment, academic integration, professional growth, and personal support. Our mission is to support the Tulane international community and facilitate cross-cultural interaction.

name: ACEing Autism
description:
ACEing Autism is on a mission for children with autism to grow, develop and benefit from social connections and fitness through affordable tennis programming, uniquely serving individual needs while filling a national void for this growing and worthy population. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: On Sundays we meet for 1 hour to teach children with Autism in the community how to play tennis. This helps them with their motor and social skills. No prior knowledge of tennis or working with special needs children is required to join.

name: Baseball Club
description: Tulane Club Baseball is a student-run organization at Tulane University in New Orleans, Louisiana. The team plays in the National Club Baseball Association (NCBA), in the Gulf Coast–East Conference along with the University of Alabama, Auburn University, Louisiana State University, Mississippi State University, and the University of Mississippi. Check out @tulanebase on Instagram for game updates and recruiting! (Or send an email to kdove1@tulane.edu)
summary: Tulane Club Baseball is a student-run organization at Tulane University in New Orleans, Louisiana. The team plays in the National Club Baseball Association (NCBA), in the Gulf Coast-East Conference.

name: Intercultural Council
description: The Intercultural Council (ICC) is a subset of the Undergraduate Student Government which works closely with the Center for Intercultural Life to serve the needs of and represent the following multicultural organizations: - Tulane University Generating Excellence Now and Tomorrow in Education (TU GENTE) - Asian American Student Union (AASU) - Indian Association of Tulane University (IATU) - Muslim Students Association (MSA) - Tulane Black Student Union (tBSU) - Tulane Chinese Students & Scholars Association (TCSSA) - Tulane University Vietnamese Association (TUVA) - TUFSA (Tulane University Filipino Student Association) - FIT (Finding Intersectionality Together) - BLSA (Black Law Student Association - Pre Law Division) - CRIT (Chinese Rights Initiative of Tulane) - SPICMACAY (Society for the Promotion of Indian Classical Music and Culture Amongst Youth) - SOAR (Students Organized Against Racism) - The goal of the Multicultural Council is to not only bring attention to the concerns of its constituents on the Undergraduate Student Government but to also foster collaboration, innovation, and a sense of common purpose amongst its organizations. Voicing for a number of underrepresented, minority populations on campus, the Intercultural Council aims to furthermore provide a safe space for multicultural students to express themselves, meet other students from similar backgrounds as their own, and discuss critical issues facing their representation on Tulane's campus.
summary: The Intercultural Council (ICC) is a subset of the Undergraduate Student Government which works closely with the Center for Intercultural Life to serve the needs of and represent the multicultural organizations on Tulane University's campus.

name: Wavelength A Cappella (Formerly known as THEM)
description: Formed in the '90s and originally known as THEM, Wavelength is Tulane's contemporary collegiate a cappella group. We perform all our own arrangements, ranging anywhere from Ariana Grande, to Stevie Nicks, to The Beatles. We have concerts at the end of the fall and spring semester, and perform at various campus events throughout the year. Our auditions are held annually at the start of the fall semester. For any questions or more information regarding auditions, booking us for performances, etc., contact us through Instagram, email, or text, linked below.
summary: Check out our Instagram @wavelength.acappella for the most up-to-date info!!

name: Tulane for UNICEF
description:
UNICEF at Tulane University is a UNICEF Campus Initiative Club affiliated to the US Fund for UNICEF (United Nations Children's Fund). Our organization promotes the rights and well-being of all youth. UNICEF is also committed to ensuring special protection for the most disadvantaged children – victims of war, disasters, extreme poverty, all forms of violence and exploitation, and those with disabilities. Tulane for UNICEF makes a difference with our goal on campus to fundraise for UNICEF, advocate for their causes, and educate our campus about UNICEF and their projects. UNICEF works in both emergency and non-emergency contexts to protect the rights of children. Every day, millions of children are denied their fundamental right to safety and security. Through UNICEF's work in more than 190 countries and territories, we save lives and build futures. Join us to help protect children's rights and development around the world!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Welcome to the Tulane Chapter Campus Initiative for UNICEF! The UNICEF Club at Tulane University is affiliated with the U.S. Fund for UNICEF (United Nations Children's Fund). Our organization promotes the rights and well-being of all youth.

name: Public Health Undergraduate Student Government
description:
The Public Health Undergraduate Student Government (PHUSG) enhances the academic and social experience of Tulane’s undergraduate public health community. We provide platforms to explore health topics beyond the classroom through events such as documentary screenings, career panels, networking nights, alumni mixers, and professional development workshops. Our signature programs—Public Health Week and the Mardi Gras Fanny Packs initiative—promote student engagement and campus wellness. Have a question or idea? Reach out to us anytime on Wavesync!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: PHUSG aim to create an enriching experience for any Tulane undergraduate public health students. We organize events such as professional panels, alumni mixers, social networking events, and hands-on workshops.

name: Chi Alpha Christian Fellowship

name: Lacrosse Club - Women's
description: Our team practices during both fall and spring semesters, but we compete mostly in the spring. We play in the Texas Women's Lacrosse League against teams including UT Austin, Baylor, Texas A&M, and more. We also play a few out of league games as well against schools like LSU and Vanderbilt. The team is no-cut, and was created so that Tulane students interested in the sport would be able to continue their love for the game in college and play at a somewhat competitive level. Please feel free to contact the team with any questions!
summary: Tulane's Women's Club Lacrosse TeamOur team practices during both fall and spring semesters but we compete mostly in the spring. We play in the Texas Women's Lacrosse League against teams like UT Austin, Baylor, Texas A&M, and Arkansas.

name: Tulane Jazbaa
description:
Jazbaa is Tulane's competitive Bollywood fusion dance team! Bollywood is a highly energetic dance form derived from India's film industry. The dance is a blend of Indian classical and traditional dance with western influences. We incorporate a wide variety of Indian dance styles to promote the culture and spread our love of dance as we travel to competitions around the nation! 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Jazbaa is Tulane's premiere competitive Bollywood fusion dance team! Bollywood is a highly energetic dance form derived from India's film industry. Jazbaa performs at on-campus events and around New Orleans while competing across the nation.

name: Crawfest
description:
Crawfest is a music and food festival held annually at Tulane University. Each year Crawfest distributes tons (yes, literally tons) of crawfish and attracts over 10,000 guests. As one of the largest student-run music and food festivals in the country, Crawfest requires a team of dedicated, creative, honest, and sharp students.

 

As a Tulane student, joining Crawfest is the best way to gain real, valuable career-relevant experiences and learn leadership skills. Within the first few months of joining Crawfest, we've had members negotiate artist contracts with top talent agencies, work on high-level sponsorship deals, plan an in-depth marketing calendar, arrange the coordination of 200+ volunteers, and organize the complex on-site festival logistics. By working on Crawfest, you'll meet like-minded people, improve your professional skillset, and add some impressive lines to your resume.

 

Learn more about some of the open positions and even apply here: https://crawfest.notion.site/Working-on-Crawfest-da150eaabaef4664b1dcb162171dc61c
 
Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Crawfest is an annual music, food, and arts festival at Tulane University. Attendees enjoy music, 20,000+ pounds of crawfish, and dozen of local vendors in the heart of New Orleans.

name: Chapel of the Holy Spirit
description: The Chapel of the Holy Spirit is an Uptown Episcopal Chapel of The Diocese of Louisiana, serving as campus ministry for Episcopal, ELCA Lutheran and Anglican students, faculty, and staff of Tulane and Loyola Universities. We worship at 10 a.m. every Sunday and Wednesdays at 5:30 before our Canterbury Club meeting. There is a free dinner for students every Thursday at 6:00 PM with our Canterbury Club student organization. We look forward to a year of programming and events for the students of both universities. We will host a silent retreat at City Park in late September and be on the lookout for our Pub Theology series at The Boot. We will also have Bible studies and popcorn theology that you can participate in throughout the year. During Football season, drop by for a hamburger or hotdog and visit our tailgate at all home games. Also, our yard is your yard. If you need a quite place to get away, just come on by, the gates are always open. We also inaugurate our commitment to learning in the Anglican tradition in the fall, celebrating Michaelmas on or near the Feast Day of St. Michael and All Angels on September 29. We also celebrate Reformation Sunday in the ELCA Lutheran tradition. If you need to contact our chaplain, Fr. Watson Lamb, he is always available for pastoral counseling or just a conversation about God and life. His number is 504-533-4916. You are invited to join us anytime at 1100 Broadway, across the street from The Boot. All are Welcome!
summary: The Chapel of the Holy Spirit is an Uptown Episcopal Chapel of The Diocese of Louisiana, serving as campus ministry for Episcopal, ELCA Lutheran and Anglican students, faculty, and staff of Tulane and Loyola Universities. We worship at 10 a.m. every Su...

description:
Beta Alpha Psi (BAPsi) is an honor organization for financial information students and professionals that recognizes excellence in the field of accounting. The Beta Nu chapter at Tulane University aims to promote networking and build relationships between accounting firms and our members. Beta Alpha Psi offers members exclusive networking opportunities with accounting professionals through events, such as Meet the Firms. Our weekly meetings feature presentations by professional services firms, including PwC, EY, Deloitte and KPMG as well as other local and regional firms. The chapter currently has around 180 members and opens for pledging every semester.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

Our singers are talented and dedicated individuals who are heavily involved in all areas of campus. TULA is a diverse and tight-knit group committed to lifting each other up and making great music!

name: After-School Newcomb Tutoring
description:
You will be paired with a local K-8 student to help with their homework, reading, etc. Tutors have the opportunity to work with the same student each week in order to build a relationship as a tutor, mentor, and friend! Work to improve grades and teach long-term study skills and goal-setting. Be a mentor/role model and help them see college in their future! We’re on campus – you don’t need a car! We also offer online services if you'd prefer to tutor from your room.

Interested in being a tutor or a sub? Email us at <ant.supervisors@gmail.com>!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: College Democrats
description:
The Tulane College Democrats work to further progressive causes and elect strong Democrats to political office. As citizens and leaders of today and tomorrow, we pledge ourselves to furthering the causes of the Democratic Party of equality and justice. We promote these ideals through voter registration drives and volunteer opportunities and supply time and energy to Democratic candidates at the grassroots level. We increase awareness and educate our community by sponsoring guest speakers, discussion groups, and political debates. We facilitate student participation in university, local, state, and national politics by bringing politicians to campus to better connect students with their leaders and the political process. Get involved in the Tulane College Democrats today! Join us as we work towards a better, more just Tulane, New Orleans, Louisiana, and America! Come to a meeting! Meetings are on Wednesdays at 7:30 PM! 

Have questions? Want to be added to our email list? Reach out to us at tulanecollegedemocrats@gmail.com or talk to an E-Board member!

President: Nathan Jones

Vice President & Acting President: Ashton Layh

Membership Director: Mekhi Stennis

Finance Director: Meli Perez

Social Media Director: Avery Deaville

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Tulane College Democrats work to further progressive causes and elect strong Democrats to political office. We call for action based on principles and values and principles and values backed by action.

Tulane Residence Hall Association promotes campus involvement through programming and advocacy initiatives, provides opportunities for leadership development with guest speakers and activities, and allows members of community governments to engage with residents.

summary: Tulane Residence Hall Association promotes the well being of students, represents residents, and provides programming that fosters connections within the Residence Halls and New Orleans community.

name: Tulane University Service-Dog Training and Education Program
description:
TUSTEP is a service organization that focuses on raising assistance dogs in conjunction with Canine Companions®!  Members have the opportunity to become a puppy sitter for our dogs, learn how to train and take care of the assistance dogs in-training, and even apply to raise an assistance dog of their own.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: TUSTEP is a service organization that focuses on raising assistance dogs in conjunction with Canine Companions. Members have opportunities to become a puppy sitter, learn about assistance dogs, join committees, and apply to be a raiser!

name: Tulane Israel Public Affairs Committee
description:
The mission of TIPAC—the Tulane Israel Public Affairs Committee—is to strengthen and expand the U.S.-Israel relationship in ways that enhance the security of the United States and Israel.

We educate campus leaders and engage with decision-makers about the bonds that unite the two countries, and how it is in America’s best interest to strengthen those bonds and help ensure that the Jewish state remains safe, strong, and secure. We also hold general body events open to all Tulane students to help them stay informed on politics. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: TIPAC is a bipartisan organization dedicated to strengthening and expanding the U.S.-Israel relationship.

name: Tulane University Broadcast Entertainment
description:
TUBE is a student run production organization with over 50 years of history. Great highlights include TUVAC, TSTV and the original Tulane Today. We provide the platform and resources for Tulane students interested in digital and analog film media. We have our own studio and we're not afraid to use it ;) It could be music, video or even AI / machine-learning. TUBE has the equipment to get it done. Go viral? Be an influencer? All TUBE potential. Many of our members already have videos with millions of views.

If you are interested in digital media or just want to pursue making your own material this is the place to be!

We have our own fully-equipped studio on campus.

Let's upgrade our Instagram Reels, Youtube Shorts and TikTok !

Auditions every semester for Singers / Dancers / Production Crew.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: TUBE is a student-run group with over 100M in member views. Want to go viral or be an influencer? Pro gear for AI, digital media, music, and video is here. Upgrade Instagram Reels, Youtube Shorts and TikTok! Auditions for Singers, Dancers, and Crew

name: Tulane AFROTC
description: Air Force ROTC is a college program offered at more than 1,100 colleges and universities across the U.S. It prepares you to become an Air Force Officer while earning a college degree. And it gives you the opportunity to get the tuition money you need. But more than that—it’s a challenge, a head start on a lifetime of success, within the Air Force and in everything you choose to do. In Air Force ROTC, you’ll make the most of your college experience. You’ll hone your time-management skills, analytical skills and physical fitness. It won’t be easy. But if you’re up to the challenge, the rewards will last a lifetime.
summary: Air Force ROTC is a college program offered at more than 1,100 colleges and universities across the U.S. It prepares you to become an Air Force Officer while earning a college degree. And it gives you the opportunity to get the tuition money you need. ...

description:
Best Buddies is a nonprofit organization that fosters one-on-one relationships between students and community members with intellectual or developmental disabilities. Our goals are to create lasting mutually beneficial friendships and to inform the community of the discrimination and challenges those with intellectual and developmental disabilities are faced with on a daily basis. We strive to create a more inclusive community for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: Women and Youth Supporting Each Other
description:
Women and Youth Supporting Each Other (WYSE) is a curriculum-based mentorship program that provides young women with the resources, information and support necessary to think critically and engage in discussions about relationships, gender and sexuality, race and racism, sexual health, their futures, and other important topics in order to lead happy and informed lives. Tulane WYSE works with middle school girls at Bricolage Academy. All woman-identifying people or individuals socialized as women or assigned female at birth are encouraged to apply!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Women and Youth Supporting Each Other (WYSE) is a curriculum-based mentorship program that provides young women with the resources and support necessary to make informed decisions about relationships, sexuality, and their futures.

name: Tulane EMS
description:
Tulane EMS is a fully licensed, free of charge ground transport ambulance service. (See operation hours and service area map on Student Health website.) When you call, an ambulance will respond with lights and sirens, along with a crew of two to four EMTs. Each crew is composed of student volunteers with a variety of roles on the truck; TEMS is a teaching organization, meaning our members begin riding as EMT students under the supervision of both a fully licensed lead technician and a EMS crew chief with several years of experience. TEMS can activate additional resources such as New Orleans EMS and the New Orleans Fire Department as necessary. It is our goal that patients receive the highest quality of care and attention with the lowest response time. Check out our Instagram page for any updates about TEMS!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Tulane EMS is a fully licensed ground ambulance service providing free of charge emergency care and transport to Tulane, Loyola and the Uptown community. See operation hours and service area map on Student Health website.

name: Aikido/Judo Club
description:
Aikido is a Japanese martial art primarily geared towards self-defense through disabling your opponent using wrist/joint locks, holds and throws aimed at redirecting the force of an attack rather than facing it head on. Judo is a Japanese martial art that focuses on self-defense through grappling, throwing, ground techniques, chokes, and submission, rather than striking. With Aikido and Judo, the outcome is less about size or strength than it is about skill and experience. A good aikidoka or judoka must be analytical and fast at the same time. Practice for all levels is at 8 PM on Tuesday and Thursday each week in Diboll Studio. No prior martial arts experience is necessary. We welcome anyone who wants to learn!

 

Promo Video
summary: Aikido and Judo are Japanese martial arts dedicated to self-defense via redirecting the force of an attack using throws, holds, joint locks, and ground grappling. We provide a space to teach and practice these arts where all levels of skill are welcome.

description:

summary: We are Tulane's premier improv comedy group! We have monthly comedy shows! Everything we perform has never been performed before and will never be performed again--all we ask is a one word suggestion. Audition! Attend! Ascend!

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc.
description:
Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated (AKA) is an international service organization that was founded on January 15, 1908, on the campus of Howard University by 16 illustrious and trailblazing women led by Ethel Hedgemon-Lyle. Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority’s mission is to cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, to promote unity and friendship among college women, to study and help alleviate problems concerning girls and women in order to improve their social stature, to maintain a progressive interest in college life, and to be of “Service to All Mankind.” AKA currently has over 360,000 graduate and undergraduate members in over 12 countries. Visit http://www.aka1908.com/ for more information on our illustrious sorority!

Omicron Psi is the Tulane chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated, chartered on April 15, 1990 by the 20 Progressive Sisters of Tulane. Through our service opportunities, bonding activities,  informative programming, and dedication to academic success, we hope to continue to uphold the values of sisterhood, scholarship, and service that our founding members and charter members stood for.  Visit https://www.omicronpsi08.com/ for more information on our Oh So Pretty chapter!
summary: The Oh So Pretty Ladies of Omicron Psi Chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated welcome you to our WaveSync profile created to keep you informed of programs and events we host on campus!

name: The Phyllis M. Taylor Center for Social Innovation and Design Thinking
description:
Taylor, founded in 2014, helps students identify their changemaking path. Our programs are grounded in the teaching, research, and practices of design thinking, social entrepreneurship, and social innovation.

Our interdisciplinary undergraduate Social Innovation and Social Entrepreneurship minor creates collaborative experiences for students and faculty across disciplines and schools.

Co-curricular programs include a residential learning community, a social venture incubator for students with ideas for social change, and professional development for social impact.

Through human centered design classes and workshops, we teach students, faculty, staff, and community members how to address problems optimistically, creatively, and quickly while turning them into inspiring “challenges”.

As an Ashoka U Changemaker Campus, we espouse the value of “everyone a changemaker” and engage  Tulane constituents, New Orleans residents, and our global community in the movement toward positive social and environmental change.
summary: Taylor, founded in 2014, helps students identify their changemaking path. Our programs are grounded in the teaching, research, and practices of changemaking design thinking, social entrepreneurship, and social innovation.

name: Tulane Organization for Global Affairs
description:
The Tulane Organization for Global Affairs (TOGA) houses Tulane University’s competitive Model United Nations team, and serves as the heart of all international affairs-related activity on campus. TOGA hosts panels, lectures, and discussions surrounding today’s most pressing international issues. TOGA has sent a delegation to compete at universities across the country, including Duke, University of Pennsylvania, Georgetown University, Emory University, and the University of Chicago. TOGA also hosts Louisiana’s only high school Model UN conference, WaveMUN, established in 2018. If you are interested in international politics, history, speech, or debate, you will make a wonderful addition to our team. We are so excited for you to join us! Roll TOGA!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

description:
GES is a club open to all Tulane students! We meet weekly in the Q-Spot (in PJ's coffee on Willow) or the Carolyn-Barber Pierre Center for Intercultural Life (in A-quad) and meetings range from social meetups to workshops to guided discussion on topics pertinent to our community. Each year we hold events during Pride Month, Trans Visibility Week, Sex Week, and Trans Awareness Week. We also host other workshops, film screenings and social spaces throughout the year. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: Green Club
description:
Founded in 1988, the Tulane Green Club remains a leading environmental organization not only at Tulane but also throughout the New Orleans community. The club continually strives to "green" the Green Wave through our efforts to make our campus more sustainable. We also educate the Tulane and New Orleans community through speakers, panels, on-campus events, and volunteering with local environmental organizations. Currently, New Orleans, the greater Southern Louisiana area, and our planet are under siege from numerous forms of environmental degradation. Ocean acidification, sea level rise, man-made global climate change, and environmental degradation threaten human existence. Please join us during the 2025-26 school year as we green, educate, and serve Tulane and the greater New Orleans community.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

Welcome to RUF Christian Fellowship at Tulane! RUF is a student organization that seeks to tell the story of Jesus, so that we may rest in his love and respond in love for God, for our neighbors, and for Tulane and New Orleans.

We invite you to come join us as we seek to know Jesus through his Word. We pray this is a place for the cynical and the confident, the bored and the passionate, the anxious and the curious to come and rest in the love of Jesus.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: The Benevolent Society for the Propagation of Assorted Tomfoolery and Other Sorts of Peculiar and Otherwise Absurd and Baffling Nonsense
description:
Ahoy!

 

This message is directed to the most intrepid and curious freshmen and any other people of the world who have found their interests piqued by The Benevolent Society for the Propagation of Assorted Tomfoolery and Other Sorts of Peculiar and Otherwise Absurd and Baffling Nonsense. You may also know us by our Ministry of Propaganda's preferred name: The J.U.G.G.L.I.N.G. C.L.U.B. We welcome you.

 

I implore any curious cats to take a little tumble down the labyrinthine rabbit hole if you are brave enough.... If you are seeking completely outdated and inaccurate information I would direct you here for lore about the mysterious origins of our noble society and here for a historical account of the bike help desk. (That first site is a titillating read.)

 

For in-dated and accurate material, please seek out our website, our Instagram page, a recent periodical profile, or our Google Maps listing. If you desire an exercise in meandering and cryptic scribing, I implore you to leave your most baffling and absurd google review for our most wondrous wall garden shed after visiting with your velocipede. 

 

**If you are truly interested in propagating baffling and absurd nonsense, I would meander to the WALL GARDEN sometime TWO HOURS after NOON on a beautiful SUNDAY afternoon. Come with a pure heart seeking true friends and flying balls. It is optional to bring a green thumb or an interest in bicycles. Join us for our merrymaking and antics galore in said garden. Not convinced? Let me paint a little picture. You, me, gaping our mouths open at the sheer beauty of our blossoming garden as our skin basks in the sun's tasty, tingly rays. We are moved by the pleasure in our eyes that we frolic, like bugs and worms amidst the grass. You hear laughing. You are happy. Additionally, if you elevate yourself to the DIBOLL parking garage rooftop--a place embroiled in mystery and intrigue--at 7 PM on MONDAYS you will find a pleasant coalescence of jugglers and flow artists. Come if you dare.**

 

If you are the type to enjoy the consumption media, here are two pieces of completely and utterly satirical art inspired by us:

Insider's brilliant work of guerilla journalism

A Satirical Short Film About Our Benevolent Society

If you want to get in CONTACT with us, the surest way is to DM the @tulanegardenguardian. Direct all other inquiries to kpickering@tulane.edu or juggling@tulane.edu, though there is the chance these missives will be lost to the ether. Please note we will only respond to emails written in a meandering and convoluted syntax. 

 

We would love to meet you, face your fears and come say hi.

 

Your loving and "democratically" elected leaders,

The Ministry of Dunces

 

Meetings: Wall garden, 2-5 PM, Sundays. Diboll rooftop, 7 PM Mondays.

Contact: ✉ juggling@tulane.edu, @tulanegardenguardian on Instagram, kpickering@tulane.edu

Website: https://thebenevolentsociety.tulane.edu/

Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/calendar/embed?src=juggling%40tulane.edu&ctz=America%2FChicago

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Benevolent Society for the Propagation of Assorted Tomfoolery and Other Sorts of Peculiar and Otherwise Absurd and Baffling Nonsense (also known by our premier acronym, The J.U.G.G.L.I.N.G. C.L.U.B.) welcomes future jugglers with many loving limbs!

We also are very active within the community, hosting fun booths for elementary and middle school students at various events, spending time with patients at hospitals, and hosting a Brain Bee for high school students. name: Pre-Medical Society
description:
The Tulane Pre-Medical Society aims to foster a sense of community among Tulane's pre-med and pre-health students while also providing them with opportunities to learn about different aspects of healthcare and participate in community service. We host many different speakers, such as medical school representatives, current medical school students, post-bac program representatives, and practicing physicians. Not only do these events allow students to learn about potential future careers, but they also bring awareness to certain health issues affecting the New Orleans community. We also aim to provide resources, advice, and peer support to pre-health students to guide them in their career journeys. We aim to be as inclusive as possible, so all of our club events are open to the public. In this way, we are also able to include those who may be unsure if they wish to pursue a career in medicine and also those who are generally interested in the topics presented. We hope to meet you at one of our events!

name: Mortar Board Honor Society
description:
Mortar Board, Inc., as an honor society, is an association of individuals selected for distinguished ability and achievement in scholarship, leadership and service, which recognizing the advantages of a national union of honor societies, has formed a national honor society, whose purpose shall be to facilitate cooperation among those societies, to contribute to the self-awareness of its members, to promote equal opportunities among all peoples, to emphasize the advancement of the status of women, to support the ideals of the college or university, to advance a spirit of scholarship, to recognize and encourage leadership, to provide service, and to establish the opportunity for a meaningful exchange of ideas as individuals and as a group.

The one hundredth chapter of Mortar Board, Alpha Sigma Sigma, was installed at Newcomb College in 1958.

Alpha Sigma Sigma was founded in 1916 at Newcomb and its alumnae were initiated into Mortar Board on March 15, 1959. Historically, Alpha Sigma Sigma was responsible for the planning, organization, and supervision of the Freshman Orientation Program, the direction of the Freshman government and serving as hostesses at University functions. As Newcomb College became Newcomb Institute, Mortar Board persisted as a premier honor society and active student organization hosting events like the Last Lecture series and “Are You Smarter Than A Fifth Grader?”

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Mortar Board is a national honor society recognizing college juniors and seniors for their exemplary scholarship, leadership, and service. Mortar Board promotes leadership development, service to colleges and universities, and active global citizenship.

name: Meditation Club
description:
Welcome to Tulane Meditation Club!

We meet every Wednesday at 6:00 p.m. in the front lawn of the Bea Field Alumni House (across the street from Wall Garden)

(Location and Time are subject to change. Join Groupme using link below for updates!)

https://groupme.com/join_group/110871327/aTppgg8U 

 

Meditation greatly helps us through life. It isn’t about trying to clear the noise within our minds, but rather, reminding ourselves that the noise isn’t our identity. We are not our minds. Meditation has many emotional and physiological benefits and its very easily accessible! you can meditate in a sacred comfortable place or while walking to class! the practice is very diverse and can look different for everybody.

 

Our club's purpose is to create a safe and inclusive space for developing our meditation skills. We invite everyone involved to co-create this community, leveraging our unique talents and interests related to meditation. Participation and membership in our organization are open to all students, regardless of their demographic identity, and are nondiscriminatory.

Peace, love, and smiles! ࿊☮︎꩜☺︎

name: Sexual Aggression Peer Hotline and Education
description:
SAPHE is a compassionate group of Tulane students dedicated to supporting survivors of sexual violence and addressing rape culture at Tulane. The purpose of SAPHE is to provide resources and support to the Tulane community through a peer-run 24/7 hotline while working to dismantle rape culture on campus through workshops, events, and outreach. SAPHE members are trained in trauma-informed care and empathetic listening so they are well-equipped to provide support and resources to any person who might need them.

 

 

To contact SAPHE's 24/7 Peer Run Hotline during the fall and spring semesters, please call 504-654-9543.

To request a workshop from SAPHE, please fill out the form here.

 

 

2025-2026 Leadership Team

Baileigh Evans - SAPHE Advisor
Zoraya Rodriguez Mowzoun - President
Eve Tellegen - Education and Outreach Coordinator 
Grace Daniels - Hotline Manager
Emma Gombos- Events Coordinator
Dahlia Weinstein - Membership Coordinator
Dior Naylor - CIL (Community Impact and Learning) Chair
 

Become a SAPHE

SAPHE APPLICATION!
Application Process/Timeline 

  • September 22: Written application goes live on Wavesync    
  • October 19 @ 11:59 pm: Written applications due on Wave-Sync  
  • October 29th: Applicants notified if they were selected for an interview    
  • November 5th-8th: 30-minute behavior-based interviews conducted on Zoom  
  • November 15th: Final decisions released 

Interest meetings:   

  • Monday, September 15, from 5:30-6:30 pm at Diboll Gallery (Third floor of Commons)  
  • Wednesday, September 24th, from 6:00 pm – 7:00 pm at LBC Conference Room 204  
  • Tuesday, October 14th from 6:00 pm – 7:00 pm via ZOOM 

For more information about SAPHE, please visit our Instagram (@tusaphe) or feel free to view this position description (https://tulane.box.com/s/hogu9du46z9gihwqbpbwqj436e43plhm). Please feel free to reach out to our President, Zoraya Rodriguez Mowzoun (zrodriguezmowzoun@tulane.edu) if you have any questions about our recruitment or selection process.

Expectations of being a SAPHE:

  • Attend weekly one-hour general body meetings
  • Attend biweekly one-hour anti-racism meetings 
  • Complete all required training: 
    • Initial 40 hours of training for new members
    • Annual 20 hours of continuing education training for returning members
  • Maintain and manage hotline protocol and responsibilities, if self-selected to take a hotline shift
  • Facilitate workshops focused on violence prevention, support, and resources
  • Participate in SAPHE sponsored events
  • Provide peer support at various campus events when requested

 

Benefits of being a SAPHE:

  • Leadership opportunities
  • Meaningful and practical work experience
  • Increase knowledge and/or skills in the following:
    • Public Speaking
    • Presentation skills
    • Violence prevention

Have any feedback and/or concerns about SAPHE? Let us know here.

To learn more, please email whp@tulane.edu
summary: SAPHE provides resources and support to the Tulane community through a peer-run 24/7 hotline, workshops, events, and outreach. To contact SAPHE's 24/7 Peer Run Hotline during the fall and spring semesters, please call 504-654-9543.

Our events have included key note speakers, films, and service learning opportunities. description: The National Pan-Hellenic Council, Inc. is a collaborative body currently comprised of nine (9) International Greek letter Sororities and Fraternities: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc., Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc., Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc., Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc., Iota Phi Theta Fraternity, Inc., Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc., Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc., Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc., and Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. NPHC organizations work concertedly to promote fraternalism and sisterhood, the unanimity of thought and action in the conduct of Greek letter collegiate fraternities and sororities, and to proactively address issues of social significance.

name: Equestrian Club
description: Welcome to the home page of the Tulane University Equestrian Team! Our program is fairly new but has been built up significantly in the past few years, and has become highly competitive in our region! We compete in Zone 5 Region 4 of the Intercollegiate Horse Show Association (IHSA) against schools such as LSU, University of Texas, Trinity University, Texas Southern University, and Rice University. In intercollegiate horse shows, the host school supplies all of the horses and competitors draw a horse at random to ride for each of their classes, so you do not need to have your own horse to join the Equestrian Team. Divisions are split up into Open, Intermediate, Novice, Advanced Walk-Trot-Canter, Beginner Walk-Trot-Canter, and Walk-Trot. Tulane Equestrian welcomes new members of ALL levels from those who have shown in BigEq classes to those who have never sat on a horse before!
summary: Welcome to the home page of the Tulane University Equestrian Team! We compete in Zone 5 Region 4 of IHSA and have become highly competitive in our region.

name: Community Service Fellows & Weatherhead Scholars
description:
The Community Service Fellows Program (CSF), established in 2007, is a four-year developmental scholarship that deeply engages incoming freshmen who performed exceptional community service in high school and commit to continued service during their college experience. This program combines practical experience, ongoing training and reflection activities, mentorship and opportunities to collaborate with other student leaders and community agencies to learn about and support sustainable community development.

 

The Weatherhead Scholars Program selects high-achieving incoming freshmen who demonstrate excellence in academics, community engagement, and leadership. Scholars are expected to uphold these standards while developing a community engagement project over the course of their four-year undergraduate tenure. In return, the Center for Public Service offers resources, mentorship for community connections, and opportunities for personal and professional growth.
summary: The Community Service Fellows and the Weatherhead Scholars Programs are scholarships for incoming freshmen, each fostering community engagement and leadership. These programs aim to develop socially aware leaders committed to addressing societal issues.

name: Tulane University Vietnamese Association
description:
The Tulane University Vietnamese Association (TUVA) is a student organization committed to its three essential pillars: culture, charity, and community. TUVA emphasizes Vietnamese culture by showcasing and promoting aspects like food, clothing, and entertainment through annual events, such as Food Festival, Tet (Vietnamese New Year) Festival, and other events and fundraising sales throughout the year. TUVA emphasizes charity by participating in the Union of North American Vietnamese Student Association’s (UNAVSA) nationwide cultural philanthropy project to raise money for charitable organizations; past examples have included the Children of Vietnam and Pacific Links Foundation. TUVA emphasizes community by creating and sustaining a close family of members on campus, both Vietnamese and non-Vietnamese. In fact, we encourage involvement and support from students of ALL ethnic and social backgrounds, and we stand in solidarity with other students of color. Off-campus, TUVA interacts with The Union of Vietnamese Student Associations Gulf Coast (UVSA Gulf Coast) to collaborate with the Vietnamese Student Associations of other schools in the region such as LSU, UNO, Loyola, and USM to name a few. In addition to UVSA, TUVA also collaborates with the Tulane Office of Multicultural Affairs (OMA) and the local Vietnamese community to provide a more extensive network for its members. Come join the TUVA family! We would love to have you!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Tulane University Vietnamese Association (TUVA) is a student organization committed to its three essential pillars: culture, charity, and community. TUVA emphasizes Vietnamese culture through festivals, community engagement, and general body meetings.

name: Kappa Alpha Order
description: Kappa Alpha Order was originally founded as Phi Kappa Chi on December 21, 1865, at Washington and Lee University in Lexington, Virginia. James Ward Wood, William Archibald Walsh, and brothers William Nelson Scott and Stanhope McClelland Scott are the founders of the fraternity. Soon after the founding, the local Virginia Beta chapter of Phi Kappa Psi protested the name "Phi Kappa Chi", due to the similarity of the names, leading Wood to change the name of the fraternity to K.A. by April 1866. Within one year, the order's ritual would be expanded upon by Samuel Zenas Ammen, who was dubbed the "practical founder". In the years that followed, the fraternity spread throughout the Southern United States, as well as other states such as California, Arizona and New Mexico. KA is one-third of the Lexington Triad, along with Alpha Tau Omega and Sigma Nu.

name: Delta Sigma Theta
description: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. is a public service organization founded on the campus of Howard University in 1913 by twenty-two dynamic women on Christian principles with an emphasis on scholarship, service, and sisterhood. Today, Delta Sigma Theta boasts a membership of over 350,000 women, making it one of the largest predominantly African-American women's organization in the world. Its membership is dedicated to the mission and vision of the founders and contributes countless hours of public service implemented through the sorority's Five-Point Programmatic Thrusts.
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. is a public service organization founded on Christian principles with an emphasis on scholarship, service, and sisterhood. Twenty-two dynamic women founded this organization on the campus of Howard University in 1913.

name: Tulane Black Student Union
description:
The purpose of tBSU, established in 1969 as The African American Congress at Tulane, is to provide all students of color guidance, comradeship, and support, while centering Black students, and giving a perspective on the history, plight, resilience, and current standing of Black people at Tulane University, at other universities and throughout the world. This organization does not bar membership based on race or ethnicity and promotes the participation of all races.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Established in 1969, the purpose of tBSU is to provide all students of color guidance, comradeship, and support, while centering Black students, and giving a perspective on the history, plight, resilience, and current standing of Black people at Tulane.

name: Sports Business Association
description:
We are a club consisted of Tulane students who are interested in pursuing a career in the sports business field. Our purpose as a club is to provide knowledge of what goes on behind the scenes in the sports world. We bring in multiple speakers each semester, ranging from different fields including sports operations, sports management, sports agency, and more. The end goal that we are working towards is to be a club that can help students connect with companies in the sports field, including professional sports teams, to help Tulane students get those hard-fought jobs and internships.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: A Network of Tulane Undergraduate Students Pursuing Careers in the Sports Industry.

name: Tulane University Gente
description:
TU Gente is committed to contributing activities and events to the larger Tulane community in order to increase Latinx/Latine students' presence and visibility on campus as well as offer opportunities to celebrate, unite and support our communities. TU Gente works to build a more interactive and aware environment on Tulane’s campus. We accomplish this by planning, partnering and co-sponsoring major events, including speakers, fundraisers, and celebrations, during the year to raise awareness about our cultures.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: TU Gente is committed to contributing activities and events to the larger Tulane community in order to increase Latinx/Latine students' presence and visibility on campus as well as offer opportunities to celebrate, unite and support our communities.

name: Tulane Hillel
description: Tulane Hillel is a non-profit community center that fosters leadership and community engagement. A leader in entrepreneurial-based programming, Tulane Hillel serves as an incubator and platform for college students to engage in the community, focusing on key issues that need to be addressed. The Tulane Hillel team is a collection of voices, backgrounds, Hebrew School experiences, ambitions and identities. It is important that those who spend their time creating Jewish community are reflective of the broad and diverse demographic that comprise our co-creators.
summary: Tulane Hillel is a non-profit community center that fosters leadership and community engagement. A leader in entrepreneurial-based programming, Tulane Hillel serves as an incubator and platform for college students to engage in the community.

name: Kappa Sigma
description: Kappa Sigma is the largest college social fraternity in the world with more than 250,000 living members, including over 17,000 undergraduates and nearly 300 chapters and colonies located throughout the United States and Canada. Founded in 1869 at the University of Virginia, Kappa Sigma International Headquarters is based in Charlottesville, Virginia.
summary: Sigma Chapter of Kappa Sigma at Tulane University

name: South Asian Graduate Association
description:
Welcome to the website of our South Asian Graduate Association (SAGA)!

We are an organization that represents and supports Tulane students from Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka. We would love to have students join us regardless of their origin. Our Association provides a platform for students from South Asian countries to connect with each other, share their experiences and support each other in their academic journeys. We undertake various activities and events such as panel discussions, cultural showcases, networking events, community service, and guest speaker events. These events help us to engage with our members and create a sense of community and belonging.

We believe that by collaborating and celebrating diversity, we can create a platform for our diverse talents and knowledge to be shared with the broader university community. Through our association, we hope to spread awareness, advocate for issues and promote cultural exchange among our members and other students at the university. 

This website serves as a hub for information about upcoming events, news and updates about SAGA. We encourage you to stay connected with us and join our association to celebrate the richness and diversity of South Asian culture and community.

Any suggestions or concerns are welcome. Thank you for visiting our site!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: SAGA provides a community of graduate & professional students who identify themselves belonging to  Indian subcontinental region also known as South Asian countries specifically Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka

description: Mission Statement The Service Learning Assistants program recruits, trains, and empowers exceptional student-leaders to help build the capacity of the Center for Public Service to ensure that every student has the opportunity to participate in transformative, meaningful and reciprocal Service Learning. About the SLA Program Service Learning Assistants (SLA) are paid student leaders who help coordinate and support service learning courses at Tulane. SLAs are typically assigned to support 2-3 service learning courses per semester, supporting faculty, students and community partners with planning, presentations, orientations, reflections and logistics.
