description:
BMES is an organization which promotes better understanding of biomedical engineering by sponsoring academic, professional, and social programs for students interested in the field of biomedical engineering.

Our mission is to increase BME retention rates through primarily first- and second- year student support. Our purpose is to act as a "home base" for all BME students (and those interested in the field), connecting them with employment, research, and volunteering opportunities.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of field of study.
summary: BMES is an organization which promotes better understanding of biomedical engineering by sponsoring academic, professional, and social programs for students interested in the field of biomedical engineering.

name: Rowing Club
description: Tulane Crew was founded in 1985. In the relatively brief time rowing has existed on campus, the crew has won three national titles, traveled across the nation to race against the finest competition available, and competed in England and Ireland in the post-season. Our rowers have qualified for World University Games, won slots on the United States national team, and raced to a silver medal at the 1988 Olympics. The team gives students an extremely engaging and fun experience at Tulane. Please message if you're interested in joining. No experience necessary!
summary: Tulane Crew was begun in 1985. In the relatively brief time rowing has existed on campus, the crew has won three national titles, traveled across the nation to race against the finest competition available, and competed in England and Ireland in the po..

name: Study Abroad & Exchange, A.B. Freeman School of Business
description: Freeman's Study Abroad & Exchange programs enable students to live and learn in another culture, whether for a semester or for a summer, and including students coming to Tulane for one or more semesters from abroad. In these immersive settings, students develop international management skills by focusing on cultural understanding and global strategies that create competitive advantages in international business.
summary: Freeman's Study Abroad & Exchange programs enable students to live and learn in another culture, whether for a semester or for a summer, and including students coming to Tulane for one or more semesters from abroad. In these immersive settings, student...

name: Community Engagement Advocates
description:
The Community Engagement Advocates (CEAs) are a team of student leaders dedicated to fostering peer-to-peer dialogue and reflection across the Tulane community. CEAs offer facilitated conversations, workshops, and programming designed to explore complex topics across social issues, global perspectives, and intercultural competencies as they intersect with civic engagement and community partnerships. Through their one-year commitment, CEAs lead dialogues on topics embedded in the classroom curriculum and the broader university experience, including but not limited to shared ancestry, history, interpersonal relationship, self-awareness and social consciousness.

Interested in bringing a dialogue to your classroom, organization or department? For groups, request a Community Engagement Advocate dialogue by submitting this form: https://tulane.co1.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_endPCkKlnEXXtVc

Please submit all requests at least three weeks in advance.

If you cannot fit a dialogue in your class schedule, or (faculty & staff) want to offer your students another option, or are an individual participant, we offer Open Sessions within the first couple weeks of every semester. You can find those under our Events when available - but spots fill up fast!
summary: The Community Engagement Advocates program is a peer-to-peer intergroup dialogue facilitation program managed by the Center for Public Service.

name: Tulane University American Marketing Association
description:
The American Marketing Association (AMA) was established in 1937 and has grown to be one of the largest marketing associations in the world with over 30,000 members and alumni who work, teach, and study within the field of marketing. AMA is a nationally recognized association with chapters at universities around the country. Our club, the Tulane University American Marketing Association (TUAMA), is a student-driven organization within the A.B. Freeman School of Business. Open to all students, members attend bi-weekly meetings. TUAMA provides its members with basic information about marketing, the marketing industry, and how to brand themselves, including building a strong network, crafting a strong résumé, and ultimately landing an internship or job. TUAMA primarily communicates this information to its members through a variety of guest speakers, including Tulane professors and New Orleans business and marketing professionals. TUAMA equips its members with practical information and skills to help them succeed in today's marketing field.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The American Marketing Association (AMA) was established in 1937 and has grown to be one of the largest marketing associations in the world with over 30,000 members and alumni who work, teach, and study within the field of marketing. AMA is a nationall...

name: Empowering Change Task Force/Health Spot at Elk Place
description: The mission of the Empowering Change Task Force (ECTF) is to create an anti-racist institution and create a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive community at all levels of the Tulane School of Social Work (TSSW),  and we are reaching out to our students, faculty, staff, alumni, and community stakeholders to join the Task Force and support our efforts. We fully recognize and acknowledge the importance and enormity of this task. We also recognize and acknowledge that to effect long-lasting systemic, structural, and institutional change at TSSW will require commitment, dedication, persistence, intentionality, and the WILL to make change happen. We look forward to the challenge of creating a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive Tulane School of Social Work, and look forward to your assistance.
summary: The mission of the Empowering Change Task Force (ECTF) is to create an anti-racist institution, and create a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive community at all levels of the Tulane School of Social Work (TSSW)

name: The Research Ambassadors
description:
The Research Ambassadors is a Recognized Student Organization that works with the Office of Undergraduate Research to manage the Tulane University Research Network, a database of researchers and healthcare professionals actively accepting undergraduates for laboratory research and pre-health shadowing experiences. The Research Ambassadors also puts on events for undergraduate students to learn more about the experiences of researchers in all disciplines and offers networking opportunities for students to expand their impact on campus and beyond.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Research Ambassadors runs the Tulane University Research Network, which helps undergraduate students connect with researchers and healthcare professionals.

name: Center for Engaged Learning & Teaching
description:
CELT's vision is that Tulane will be a leader among its peers for:

  • elevating excellence in teaching
  • the wide-spread use of evidence-based pedagogies
  • promoting the synergies between research and teaching
  • the collaborative networks of learning-based entities across campus

 

Guided by this vision statement, 

CELT is committed to fulfill it's Core Purpose:

To create meaningful learning through exceptional teaching. 

 

CELT is committed to supporting all Tulane faculty as they prepare or continue to teach. Faculty includes full time, part time, tenure and non-tenure track positions, grad students, adjuncts who are currently or will be teaching at the wide range of campus locations and programs. With this aim in mind, we offer pedagogy workshops, guest lecturers, book clubs, one-on-one consultations, a lending library, peer observations, and myriad other resources, services, and programs available for all teaching focused professionals. 

We are still dedicated to enhancing the student experience at Tulane not only through engaged teaching techniques but also through the creation of small intellectual communities.
summary: CELT is committed to supporting all Tulane faculty as they prepare or continue to teach.

name: Soccer Club - Women's
description: Tulane Women's Club Soccer is a student-run club soccer program serving female-identifying students, faculty, staff, and alumni of Tulane University, as well as current students of Loyola University. The team competes in the Texas Collegiate Soccer League against schools such as LSU, Rice, University of Houston, Texas A&M Corps, and Sam Houston State University and practices biweekly.
summary: Tulane Women's Club Soccer is a student-run club soccer program serving female-identifying students, faculty, staff, and alumni of Tulane University, as well as current students of Loyola University.

name: Public Health Society
description:
Public Health Society aims to create a community within the Tulane Undergraduate School of Public Health. We aim to raise awareness of public health issues on and off campus and to explore what public health means at Tulane and in New Orleans. We provide opportunities to get involved on campus, participate in community service, and be a part of planning fun and community building activities. We want to connect all the undergraduate PH students and create an organization where we can all come together to discuss our field of study!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Chapter
description:
As the largest co-ed professional law fraternity in the United States, Phi Alpha Delta represents passions for advocacy, improvement, and change. Our diverse brotherhood fosters a range of legal interests including, but not limited to: corporate law, policy, international law, immigration law, real estate law, civil rights law, environmental law, criminal law, tax law, maritime law, and overall litigation. With membership, we offer professional and academic benefits such as LSAT tutoring, networking events, law school admissions panels, dinner with lawyers, speaker events, court watch, professional and academic workshops, as well as other benefits like social and community service events.

name: The Tulane Hullabaloo
description:
We are The Tulane Hullabaloo, the eyes and ears of the Tulane community. We strive to deliver honest and timely information through our website, www.tulanehullabaloo.com, and our monthly print issue, which comes out monthly.

 

We are continually looking for staff writers and photographers, who make up the core of our personnel. Students are highly encouraged to apply at the beginning of each semester to take advantage of group training and community-building events. Late applications may be accepted if students express special interest. To check for open positions on our General Board, please visit our website or contact hullstories@tulane.edu directly. 

 

Note that requesting to join on Wavesync does not constitute an official request to join The Hullabaloo's staff. Interested students should fill out an application form, made public on our website and social media pages, at the beginning of the fall and spring semesters, or reach out to a member of the Managing Board directly. 

 

Contact hullstories@tulane.edu with any and all questions or concerns. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Tulane Hullabaloo is your college newspaper and the eyes and ears of the Tulane community.

name: Zeta Beta Tau
description: The mission of Zeta Beta Tau Fraternity (ZBT) is to foster and develop in its membership the tenets of its Credo: Intellectual Awareness, Social Responsibility, Integrity and Brotherly Love, in order to prepare its members for positions of leadership and service within their communities. Mindful of its founding in 1898 as the Nation's first Jewish Fraternity, ZBT will preserve and cultivate its relationships within the Jewish community. Since 1954, ZBT has been committed to its policy of non-sectarian Brotherhood, and values the diversity of its membership. ZBT will recruit and initiate men of good character, regardless of religion, race or creed who are accepting of these principles.

name: Interfraternity Council
description: The Tulane Interfraternity Council (IFC) is the governing body of the social fraternities on campus and is comprised of representatives from each of its member organizations. The council has the power to formulate policy and regulate member organizations, and it serves as a liaison between the fraternities and the campus and community. IFC also promotes interfraternity spirit through programs such as Greek Weekend, facilitates educational and public relations endeavors, and coordinates recruitment efforts. Most IFC member organizations are, or have been, a part of the North-American Interfraternity Conference.
summary: The Tulane Interfraternity Council (IFC) is the governing body of the social fraternities on campus and is comprised of representatives from each of its member organizations. The council has the power to formulate policy and regulate member organization.

name: Ballroom Dance Club
description: Ballroom Dancing is a purely recreational group with a focus on having fun! We meet weekly on Thursdays at 8:00-10:00 PM to learn a variety of dance styles. Meetings are located in the Reily Lakeside Room. You do not need to bring a partner and no experience is required.
summary: Ballroom Dancing is a purely recreational group with a focus on having fun! We meet weekly on Thursdays at 8:15-10:15 PM to learn new dance styles.  Meetings are located in the Reily Lakeside Room. No experience needed.

name: Swim Club
description:
Welcome to Tulane Swim Club! We currently practice on Mondays & Wednesdays from 6:30-8:00pm. Any changes will be shared through our GroupMe and posted here.  

Please email tulaneswimclub@gmail.com if you're interested in joining! We are open to all swimmers and are looking to grow the swim community here at Tulane. If you're just starting out or advanced and want to meet fellow swimmers, this is the place for you! Check out our Instagram too @tulaneswimclub !
summary: Welcome to Tulane Swim Club! We currently practice twice a week at the Reily Center and attend meets 1-2x per semester. Email tulaneswimclub@gmail.com or check out our Instagram @tulaneswimclub for more info or if you're interested in joining!

name: NJBeats
description: We are NJBeats A Cappella, a group of students who love to make music and sing! NJBeats is the first Jewish-founded a cappella group at Tulane University and in Louisiana, and we sing a wide variety of songs, from pop music to rock to Hebrew songs! We perform both on campus and locally in New Orleans, such as at annual school events and Jewish community centers such as Hillel. We also arrange some of our own pieces! Artists we've covered in the past include Britney Spears, Fleetwood Mac, Bruno Mars, Queen, Pentatonix, and many more. NJBeats is open to all who love music and singing with friends! :)
summary: A group of singers who love making music together, covering a wide variety of artists of our choice and performing around New Orleans! We're the first Jewish-founded group in Louisiana, and we perform in Jewish communities both on and off campus!

name: Newcomb Department of Music
description: The mission of the Music Department at Tulane University is to train and inspire aspiring professionals as well as enthusiasts for lifelong involvement in the various facets of music. This instruction is offered by talented performers, historians, ethnomusicologists, theorists, composers and technologists, who honor and build upon the outstanding music tradition of the department. By giving freely of these strengths and resources, and offering educational guidance and enriching performances on and off campus, the influence of the department resonates in the greater New Orleans community. Rooted in the uniquely dynamic musical past and present of the city, it seeks to contribute to this cultural life, while fostering an understanding of the broader practice of music-making worldwide.
summary: The mission of the Music Department at Tulane University is to train and inspire aspiring professionals as well as enthusiasts for lifelong involvement in the various facets of music.

name: Tulane Special Olympics
description:
The Tulane University Special Olympics (TUSO) provides weekly sports practices and community events for individuals aged 8 and older with intellectual disabilities. We offer three volunteer-led sports sessions each week: volleyball, flag football, and basketball. These sessions allow members of the Tulane community to participate in meaningful service through athletic engagement, community building, and fostering friendships. In addition to our sports programming, we host special events throughout the year designed to strengthen the TUSO community and offer enjoyable opportunities for our athletes.

Want to get involved? Send us an email at tulanespecialolympics@gmail.com and check out our linktree for more information! http://linktr.ee/tulanespecialolympics1

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: TUSO provides year-round sports training and athletic competitions in a variety of sports in addition to special events for individuals with intellectual disabilities. Join TUSO to participate in football, basketball, soccer, and swimming practices!

CAEPH has pioneered quality distance learning since the first class was delivered in January 1994. Since then, our distance learning programs have grown with new technology, enhanced degree offerings in the occupational health and safety areas and disaster management, added graduate certificates and been a leader in high quality distance education.

name: Alpha Epsilon Phi
description: Alpha Epsilon Phi is proud to build on the vision of our seven Jewish founders and the strong foundation of our first one hundred years. To date we have chartered over 100 chapters and initiated over 80,000 members. We provide a well-rounded, vibrant sorority experience for our collegiate and alumnae members focusing on sisterhood, community service and personal growth. We are a Jewish sorority, but not a religious organization, with membership open to all college women, regardless of religion, who honor, respect and appreciate our Jewish identity and are comfortable in a culturally Jewish environment. Our mission and core values statements guide our sorority activities and serve as the basis for our collegiate and alumnae experiences. MISSION STATEMENT: The mission of Alpha Epsilon Phi Sorority is to inspire and support exemplary women dedicated to friendship and a lifelong commitment to Alpha Epsilon Phi while building on the vision of our Jewish founders. CORE VALUES STATEMENT: Above all else, Alpha Epsilon Phi inspires exemplary women who are: Enriched by sisterhood and unconditional friendships, Dedicated to selfless service and inspiring others, and Committed to intellectual growth and personal development. As a lifelong member of Alpha Epsilon Phi, I will: Respect our shared heritage and traditions, Exhibit high ideals and moral character, Fulfill expectations and responsibilities of membership, and Continually exemplify the values of beauty, strength and wisdom as embodied by the three columns of our insignia.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Phi is proud to build on the vision of our seven Jewish founders. To date we have chartered over 100 chapters and initiated over 80,000 members. We provide a well-rounded, vibrant sorority experience for our collegiate members.

name: Trash to Treasure
description:
T2T is a student-led sustainability project dedicated to reducing waste and engaging Tulane students in community service.

At the end of every school year, Tulane students throw away tons of reusable dorm room items. With the help of volunteers, T2T redirects these supplies from the dumpster and sells them back at discounted prices to students during fall move-in. T2T donates all profits to Greater New Orleans-based social and environmental nonprofits.

Throughout the school year, T2T collaborates with other student orgs and community partners to host donation drives, educational tabling, and other events to engage students in local sustainability efforts.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: Capoeira Club
email: President Landon Lee: llee8@tulane.edu / Treasurer Charles Kwak: kkwak@tulane.edu / Secretary Zoey Ocampo:  zocamposobkoviak@tulane.edu
description:
GroupMe Link

Capoeira is an Afro-Brazilian martial art that combines elements of both fighting and dance. Created by African slaves in Brazil aiming to train to fight by disguising it as a dance, Capoeira is played by two people at a time in a "roda" a circle formed by the other Capoeiristas who play instruments and sing while the two people are playing to the rhythm of the music. Capoeira is not only a great way to learn a unique martial art, but also a great workout and a way to learn about Brazilian culture. No experience is necessary!

description: The Tulane University School of Medicine Medical Student Government (MSG) is the governing body for all student-run organizations at the School of Medicine and represents the TUSOM student body within the larger Tulane University community and the city of New Orleans. MSG consists of annually elected MSG Officers – President (4th year), VP (3rd year), Treasurer (3rd or 4th Year), Secretary (2nd year), Technology Officer, MSG class representatives, the class presidents, and the presidents of each recognized club or organization. MSG is a member of the Graduate and Professional Student Association (GAPSA) of Tulane University.

name: Celia Scott Weatherhead School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine Student Government Association
description:
The Tulane University Celia Scott Weatherhead School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine is the only American combined school of public health and tropical medicine and the oldest American school of tropical medicine. The school's mission is to advance public health knowledge, promote health and well-being, and prevent disease, disability, and premature mortality. This is accomplished through education of public health professionals, scientific research of problems, partnerships, and service to the global public health community. A leader in global health, the school is comprised of seven academic departments: biostatistics and data science, environmental health sciences, epidemiology, health policy and management, international health and sustainable development, social, behavioral, and population sciences, and tropical medicine and infectious disease. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Student Government Association of the Celia Scott Weatherhead School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine is the gathering body of all WSPH graduate students.

name: WTUL New Orleans 91.5
description:
WTUL is New Orleans' only student-run progressive radio station, featuring some of the best progressive, local, jazz, classical, and cheez music out there. We broadcast live 24/7 every day of the year and create and sponsor events with local record labels, bands and venues.

We can be found in G07 of the LBC or on our social medias listed below. We will be accepting new DJ apprentices in the fall, but if you have any questions you can reach out to wtul.gm@gmail.com! But you do not have to be a DJ to help our station, we're happy to share our other opportunities with you if you email us.

Thank you so much for your interest in WTUL, and don't forget to tune in on our website wtulneworleans.com!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: Tulane Cycling & Triathlon
description:
Tulane Cycling & Triathlon is the Collegiate USA Cycling and Collegiate USA Triathlon team officially representing Tulane locally, regionally, and nationally. TCT is a non-profit, student-run Club Sports program serving Tulane students, faculty, staff, alumni, and supporters in the New Orleans area. The club promotes all forms and levels of cycling and triathlon, with a particular focus on collegiate competition. These sports are traditionally, very cost-prohibitive to participate in, so we have a dedication to welcoming all by supplying bikes and other very expensive equipment to our members for use. We also have a private facility on Tulane's Broadway campus that all members get 24/7 Splash Card access to! If you've ever wanted to get into cycling or triathlon, or want to complete an IronMan, this is the way!

 

TCT is Tulane's official Collegiate member team of USA Cycling, and competes as part of the Southeastern Collegiate Cycling Conference (SECCC) and/or South Central Collegiate Cycling Conference (SCCCC).

 

Additionally, TCT is Tulane's official Collegiate member team of USA Triathlon, and competes as part of the South-Midwest Conference.

 

Membership is open to all interested Tulane University undergraduate, graduate, and professional school students, as well as other Tulane affiliates. For more information about joining, join via WaveSync or check us out on LinkTree or our Facebook group.

 

We also have a public Facebook page where we post pictures, race reports, and other interesting information about the team! Follow us here: https://www.facebook.com/TulaneCycling/

 

You can also email us at cycling@tulane.edu or triathlon@tulane.edu
summary: Tulane Cycling & Triathlon is a Collegiate USAC & USAT team focused on racing. TCT is a non-profit, student-run Club Sports program serving Tulane students, faculty, staff, alumni, and supporters in the New Orleans area. We are devoted to welcoming all!

name: Spoon University at Tulane
description:
The purpose of Spoon University is to provide college students with the chance to learn, share and talk about food via an online food publication and to build a community around food. Ultimately, the mission is to help college students eat intelligently. Spoon University has three main goals: (1) to empower students to create something meaningful and long-lasting on campus (2) to give students experience in food, journalism, online media and marketing (3) to create a strong community on campus focused on food.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: Asian American Student Union
description:
Welcome to the Asian American Student Union (AASU)! AASU's mission is to promote and support the Asian American community on campus by celebrating the diversity of cultures at Tulane and facilitating an open environment for conversations.
 
Throughout the year, we host various events where students can socialize, experience various Asian cultures, and get involved in the community. While past years have focused primarily on organizing social events, we are now looking to expand by reaching out to the community and providing meaningful experiences for our members in some form. We continuously strive to encompass all Asian and Pacific Island cultures, but this is definitely always a work in progress.
 
Additionally, our organization is NOT Asian members only. Anyone with an interest in learning about AASU and our community is welcome! We strive to make AASU a welcoming and enriching organization so please feel free to submit any questions or concerns. Hope to see you all at our events!
 
Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: Freeman Graduate Business Council
description:
The Freeman Graduate Business Council (GBC) serves the academic, professional, and personal interests of its membership. All graduate students are members of the GBC and are represented by student-elected officers. The GBC facilitates interaction and communication between the business graduate student body, the administration, the faculty, and the Tulane community in order to support the improvement of the Freeman School of Business graduate programs. The GBC strives to accomplish this mission by: 1. Marketing its students 2. Helping to foster a positive learning environment 3. Facilitating mentoring and relationship building opportunities GBC also serves as the “umbrella” organization to other graduate business organizations. The GBC reports to the Graduate and Professional Student Association (GAPSA), which is comprised of representatives from each of Tulane’s six Graduate schools. GAPSA is responsible for addressing issues which affect graduate and professional students university wide, and for allocating budgets for all graduate and professional student organizations.

description:
The Well for Health Promotion is a great place for health information, education and support. One of its greatest resources is the Peer Health Educator program! Tulane University Peer Health Educators (TUPHEs) are a dedicated and fun group of students here to help you by putting on health-related programs and helping provide resources relevant to your life.

Every TUPHE is a NASPA Certified Peer Health Educator, so we've got the training we need to help you make informed health decisions. We cover a wide range of health promotion topics within The Well's focus areas from the peer-to-peer lens- let us tell you what we know!

name: Philosophy Club
description:
The Tulane Philosophy Club hosts regular, café-style discussions and lectures led by Tulane professors, graduate students, and other knowledge seekers on topics from Buddhism to political philosophy to metaphysical inquiries. Other activities include film screenings, book clubs, and panel discussions.  Starting in Fall 2025, the Club began sponsoring an annual undergraduate conference in philosophy (https://liberalarts.tulane.edu/philosophy/news-events/tulane-undergraduate-philosophy-conference)

We recently formed a Medical Ethics branch, which will hold meetings and plan activities centered on ethical topics in medicine and related areas. Students interested in this should contact Chara Kokkiou, Faculty Advisor for the Medical Ethics Branch, at ckokkiou@tulane.edu. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students, and is open to all students regardless of demographic identity. We welcome diverse perspectives, ideas, and arguments on topics in all areas of philosophy.
summary: The Tulane Philosophy Club hosts regular, café-style discussions led by Tulane professors, graduate students, and other knowledge seekers on topics from Buddhism to political philosophy. Panels and, of course, movie nights are also common meetings.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description:
We are Tulane's premier co-ed business fraternity.

Since its formation on October 5, 1904, at New York University, Alpha Kappa Psi has been the oldest, most prestigious business fraternity. Through its core values of brotherhood, knowledge, integrity, service, and unity, AKPsi is recognized as the premier developer of principled business leaders. With nearly 240,000 members, AKPsi has expanded chapters to more than 300 campuses worldwide.

The Pi Chi Chapter at Tulane University in New Orleans, Louisiana, was founded in 1999 and re-established in 2004. With over 130 active members pursuing the fraternity's core values, this chapter has become one of the largest AKPsi chapters in the country. The Pi Chi Chapter prides itself on the many events that it offers, including Professional Development, Speaker Series, Brotherhood, Fundraising, and Community Service events.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: We are the Pi Chi chapter of Alpha Kappa Psi, a prestigious business fraternity founded in 1904. AKPsi is recognized as the premier developer of principled business leaders through its core values of brotherhood, knowledge, integrity, service, and unity.

The purpose of this organization shall be to assist the Newcomb Institute and their organizations in their endeavors to uplift students of all races, ethnicities, religions, genders, socioeconomic statuses, sexual orientations, disabilities, and years in school. Newcomb Senate shall serve as the liaison between the past and present Newcomb Senate Body of Tulane University, and it shall seek to incorporate new efforts, which are necessary to support students today, with the traditions and spirit long-held Newcomb College. A primary aim of Newcomb Senate is to bring together all Newcomb College Institute organizations and facilitate interdisciplinary conversations.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

description:
Tulane Undergraduate Mock Trial is open to all undergraduate students. Each year, our program prepares both a prosecution/plaintiff and a defense for a fictitious court case provided by the American Mock Trial Association. We then compete across the country in a competitive environment. We are currently seeking new competing members and are open to all. Students do not necessarily have to be pre-law or have mock trial experience. All members of the team benefit from improved public speaking skills, critical thinking skills, and writing skills. If interested, please contact rtoranto@tulane.edu to be added to the mailing list for updates about fall tryouts and more information! 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

description:
Tulane Women in Business helps students of all genders and majors network with professionals, foster a forum for collaboration, and promote equality in the workplace. In this organization, you can discover your future career in the business world. 

 

IF YOU WANT TO JOIN do not email the President. Add us on Wave Sync and she will add you to the Outlook mailing list. Also follow our Instagram (@tulanewib) and Facebook. 

 

Most meetings are individual in nature with lecturers catered to one or a few business/related majors (Ex: Finance + Accounting, Marketing + Communications), so you can join anytime and go to any meetings that interest you. We recommend going to as many as possible to network with lecturers and members and explore subjects pertaining to all of the business school majors.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: Tulane Food Recovery Network
description:
The Tulane Food Recovery Network fights food waste at Tulane and feeds the community. As a network, our goal is to remove barriers to food access by connecting leftovers with people in need. As volunteers, we keep a steady flow of good food out of landfills, reducing emissions and contributing to sustainable food access. By diverting food waste, we ensure that Tulane's food resources are made available to all students and re-invested in New Orleans communities, combatting food insecurity on campus and beyond. We primarily donate untouched food from the Malkin Sacks Commons to the New Orleans Mission and are always looking to broaden our impact. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Fighting food waste, Feeding people.

name: Alumni Career & Professional Development
description: You’re part of the Tulane University network for life. That means you have access to professional development resources for wherever you are in your career. From recent grads to alumni approaching retirement, we are here to help you enjoy lifelong success. We’ve helped thousands of alumni in their careers through career counseling, networking opportunities, workshops and so much more. Most services are free to Tulane alumni.

name: Global Community Health and Behavioral Sciences
shortName: Global Community Health and Behavioral Sciences
description:
Faculty in the Department of Global Community Health and Behavioral Sciences (GCHB) conduct community-based research and prepare future generations of highly-skilled and culturally competent public health professionals to design and implement public health programs that address health disparities - and the social and behavioral factors underlying these disparities - in partnership with domestic and international organizations and communities. 

The GCHB dept. is also home to the new Violence Prevention Institute (VPI). The VPI is a new entity at Tulane University designed to foster interdisciplinary collaborations among students, faculty, staff, alums, and community partners interested in preventing violence from multiple angles in New Orleans and beyond. Learn more at violenceprevention.tulane.edu!
summary: Faculty in the Department of Global Community Health and Behavioral Sciences (GCHB) conduct community-based research and prepare future generations of highly-skilled and culturally competent public health professionals to design and implement public he...

name: Title IX
description: The Title IX office is dedicated to committed fostering a learning environment free from sex discrimination where all individuals can thrive. The office's work includes coordinating the University's efforts to prevent and effectively respond to all forms of sex discrimination (including gender identity and sexual orientation discrimination) and sexual misconduct impacting community members, including sexual assault, sexual exploitation, sexual harassment, dating and domestic violence, and stalking. This work is done in compliance with applicable laws, including Title IX and the Violence Against Women Act, all of which are coordinated by the Assistant Provost for Title IX Compliance and Education. The Title IX office also maintains the University’s comprehensive program designed to prevent sexual misconduct and remediate the effects of sexual misconduct on victims and the greater Tulane community.
summary: Tulane's Title IX office works to prevent and respond to sex discrimination, sexual harassment, and sexual violence.

name: Tulane University for Israeli Culture
description:
Our goal is to be a campus resource for everything Israel-related! We aim to strengthen Tulane's connection with Israel by providing a forum for students to congregate and explore Israeli culture, including but not limited to current events, history, and culture. We share a goal to educate the student body and the larger community about Israel, exploring the diverse cultures that make up the region, regardless of political, religious, or other dividing factors.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: We are a non-partisan, non-religious student organization. Our goal is to be the campus resource for Israeli cultural education, to connect students to Israel-related resources, and to be a space safe to celebrate Israeli culture!

name: Tulane International Society
description:
Welcome to the Tulane International Society! We focus on bridging the gap between the international community and the rest of Tulane's campus by hosting off-campus trips, student-led panels, and faculty conversations.
 
Each semester, we host different events on campus (and throughout the city whenever possible) to expose international and exchange students to the culture of New Orleans and countries around the world, while making new friends. In the past, students experienced New Orleans through Pelicans games, trips to City Park, and popular city festivals. Additionally, we offer student-led panels and conversations with faculty to further explore international topics.
 
We welcome anyone interested in TIS, the international community at Tulane, and the culture of New Orleans and other countries! We hope to provide a supportive organization and hope to see you at our events! Please join this organization by joining our WaveSync page, which will you put you on our emailing list, and following our Instagram page, @tis_tulane!
 
Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Welcome to the Tulane International Society! We focus on bridging the gap between the international community and the rest of Tulane's campus by hosting off-campus trips, student-led panels, and faculty conversations. Hope to see you at our events!

name: Tulane University Libraries
description:
Tulane University Libraries consist of four physical locations across two campuses in one of the most vibrant and diverse cities in North America. The Libraries serve Tulane’s nine schools and one undergraduate college with over 4.6 million books and physical items; 1.2 million eBooks; nearly 200,000 online journals; 900 databases; and distinctive collections that focus on the many facets of New Orleans’s music, history, and culture, including early jazz; regional history; literary culture; carnival; food and drink; and the architecture of the city and Gulf South region. The Libraries host the Tulane Digital Library and an institutional repository and publish several OpenAccess journals. Tulane’s Latin American Library (LAL) holds one of the most impressive collections in the world of materials on the history and culture of Central and South America and the Caribbean. The LAL also serves as the Secretariat for the Seminar on the Acquisition of Latin American Library Materials (SALALM), an international association of librarians, archivists, book dealers, book professionals, scholars, and students interested in collecting, preserving, and providing access to Latin American, Caribbean, Iberian, and Latinx information resources in all formats. Matas Library of the Health Sciences in downtown New Orleans serves students and faculty in the Schools of Medicine and of Public Health and Tropical Medicine as well as the Tulane National Primate Center in Covington, LA.

The Tulane Libraries are made up of ca. 110 full-time staff and librarians. We are members of the Association of Research Libraries (ARL), the Association of Southeastern Research Libraries (ASERL), the Center for Research Libraries (CRL), the Coalition for Networked Information (CNI), HathiTrust, the Digital Library Federation (DLF), and other organizations. The Libraries’ values include a commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion in our leadership and professional practice.
summary: Membership is limited to staff of Tulane University Libraries.

name: Student Bar Association
description:
SBA is the student government organization of the law school and has representatives from every class. We perform three functions (1) we serve as an umbrella group designed to financially support student organizations and journals, (2) we act as a liaison between students, faculty and the administration, and (3) we create activities for students, including parties and community service events. The SBA is a member of the Law Student Division of the American Bar Association.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: SBA is the student government organization of the law school and has representatives from every class. We perform three functions (1) we serve as an umbrella group designed to financially support student organizations and journals, (2) we act as a liai..

name: Green Bull Investment Banking Group
description:
Green Bull Investment Banking Group, a selective student-run undergraduate program at Tulane University's A.B. Freeman School of Business, provides its members with essential skills and strategies to position oneself for opportunities in investment banking. The program offers a Freshmen Development Program, focused on advancing freshmen with an introduction to investment banking and professional development, including resume workshops, networking best practices, and mentorship. In the program’s Investment Banking Development Program, sophomores targeting a career in investment banking attend weekly meetings focused on technical and behavioral preparation and out-of-classroom networking and experiential learning opportunities. Junior and senior members serve as mentors to younger students and take part in continuing education.

 

Green Bull offers:

  • Technical & Behavioral Interview Training
  • Upperclassman & Alumni Mentorship
  • New York City Networking Trip
  • Guest Speakers
  • Outsourced Professional Trainings (e.g., Wall Street Prep)
  • Collaborative Peer Environment
  • Resume and LinkedIn Workshops
  • Current Event Discussions
  • Mock Interview Practice with Feedback
  • Access to Expanded Alumni Network

Applications will open in the first week of school in Winter 2026. Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Green Bull is a student-run undergraduate organization at Tulane University's A.B. Freeman School of Business, providing its members with essential skills and strategies to well-position themselves for opportunities in investment banking.

name: CPS Petitions for 2nd Tier Service Credit
description: Tulane students and faculty are able to petition for a number of public service projects that, if approved, will fulfill the 2nd tier public service graduation requirement. Petitions for service independent studies, service honors theses, transfer service credit, and public service abroad may be submitted through the CPS Petitions for 2nd Tier Service Credit Wavesync portal.
summary: Tulane students and faculty are able to petition for a number of public service projects that, if approved, will fulfill the 2nd tier public service graduation requirement. Petitions for service independent studies, service honors theses, transfer serv...

name: Compassion in Action at Tulane
description:
Compassion in Action at Tulane is an all-inclusive place for students to learn about and practice mindfulness. We apply this knowledge to better our surrounding community and incorporate values of meditation and compassion to oneself and others in everyday lives. We support students' academic and professional spheres and develop a community on Tulane’s campus that addresses wellness and mindfulness. Through outreach and volunteer events we aim to help the Tulane and greater New Orleans community.

If interested in joining - please join or Wavesync page or email neng@tulane.edu!

Meetings are bi-weekly on Tuesdays at 7:30PM. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Inspired by discussions involving compassion in both professional and personal life, Compassion in Action at Tulane is an all-inclusive sanctuary for students to learn about and practice mindfulness.

name: Event Services
description: Our event management team strategizes, plans, and implements high-impact events and special projects for internal and external audiences. We work closely with university administration, faculty, staff, students and alumni to maximize opportunities to celebrate Tulane University’s accomplishments and generate goodwill for the university. In addition to managing projects campus wide, we also offer support and guidance to the campus community, including full-service event planning, answering protocol questions and providing resources to ensure a successful event.
summary: Our event management team strategizes, plans, and implements high-impact events and special projects for internal and external audiences. We work closely with university administration, faculty, staff, students and alumni to maximize opportunities to c...

name: Graduate and Professional Student Association
description:
The Graduate and Professional Student Association (GAPSA) is the unifying body of the graduate and professional divisions of Tulane University. GAPSA develops and furthers the scholastic, professional, social, and public service interests of all graduate and professional students by promoting an understanding amongst students, faculty, staff, and administrators. GAPSA also facilitates inter-division communication and coordinates activities with the undergraduate student government. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Graduate and Professional Student Association (GAPSA) is the unifying body of the graduate and professional divisions of Tulane University.

name: Tikkun Olam Makers _Biomedical Innovation for Global Impact
description:
We are an organization focused on using human-centered design to create innovative solutions to global health problems.  It is part of a global movement (Tikkun Olam Makers) of communities that creates and disseminates affordable solutions to neglected challenges of people living with disabilities, the elderly and the poor. We try to approach the design process with fun and engaging ways that may promote growth of engineering skills over the year

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: We are an organization focused on using human-centered design to create innovative solutions to global health problems, especially neglected challenges for people living with disabilities, elderly and the poor.

name: School of Professional Advancement Student Organization
description:
The Tulane University School of Professional Advancement is dedicated to meeting the needs of working adult students. Meet fellow students, including both career-changers, seasoned professionals, and add a little fun to your university experience. We offer online and evening courses to fit your busy schedule. Our dedicated faculty brings both academic qualifications and years of experience in the field to their classrooms. In joining us, you are connected to our strong alumni network and to Tulane University—an institution known globally for excellence in higher education.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The School of Professional Advancement is dedicated to meeting the needs of working adults and part-time students. Meet fellow students with similar interests and goals, gain leadership experience, and add a little fun to your university experience.

name: Aurora Leadership Academy
description:
Aurora Leadership Project, previously CACTUS Academy, is designed for first-year Tulane undergraduate and transfer students who have an interest in social activism and a desire to develop skills and knowledge that will prepare them for social justice work during and after their time at Tulane. Aurora provides introductory training on a broad spectrum of issues in the social justice arena such as gender, sexuality, race, social services, legal rights, and community organizing, among others. In addition, participants are exposed to social justice advocates working on the Tulane campus and throughout the New Orleans community.

There is an application process to be a part of this program. The application is open from 8/26 - 9/10.
summary: Aurora is designed for first-year Tulane undergraduate and transfer students who have an interest in social activism and a desire to develop skills and knowledge that will prepare them for social justice work during and after their time at Tulane.

name: Circle K Club of Tulane University
description:
The Circle K Club of Tulane University (TCK) is a service organization geared towards community service, leadership, and fellowship. TCK is a chapter of Circle K International - the world's largest student-led collegiate service organization. Members take part in volunteering in events both on campus and in the community, participate in both community and international projects, and, in the process, form new friendships and gain the knowledge and skills to become leaders. This is a really great organization to join in order to meet new people who have the same love for community service as you do! You can meet upperclassmen and members of the New Orleans Kiwanis for networking opportunities!

Join our GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/89097880/k5MNL1GO

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

description: The Newcomb Art Museum of Tulane University builds on the Newcomb College legacy of education, social enterprise, and artistic experience. Presenting inspiring exhibitions and programs that engage communities both on and off campus, the Museum fosters the creative exchange of ideas and cross-disciplinary collaborations around innovative art and design. The Museum preserves and advances scholarship on the Newcomb and Tulane art collections. The Museum is always free and open to the public and presents several engaging programs and events throughout the year.

name: Team One Love at Tulane
description:
One Love is a national healthy relationship foundation founded in honor of Yeardley Love, a senior lacrosse player at University of Virginia who was murdered by her ex-boyfriend. One Love aims to educate and raise awareness about all types of relationship abuses in varying relationships. The peer-facilitated workshops engage with students to give them resources to prevent dangerous situations from arising.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: One Love is a national foundation created in honor of Yeardley Love, a senior lacrosse player at the University of Virginia who was murdered by her ex-boyfriend. One Love aims to educate and raise awareness about all types of relationship abuse.

We plan student art workshops, invite artists to come talk on campus, visit gallery openings and museums, and engage with the local art community. This year, we have new workshops planned and weekly meetings where you can bring your art, use our materials and make art with your friends! This is a great opportunity to meet people with common interests! No need to be an art major/minor, everyone is welcome! Join today! :)

Our Mission

To connect ambitious students to the knowledge, resources, and community they need for successful careers in entertainment industries.

Our Goals

  1. To bring industry leaders to Tulane’s campus for speaking events and networking opportunities.

  2. To build students’ resumes by organizing and executing eye-catching projects.

  3. To foster a supportive, tight-knit community for aspiring artists, athletes, and entertainment professionals.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

The Newcomb Prison Project is dedicated to educating the Tulane community about the criminal justice system and is actively pursuing efforts of criminal justice and prison reform through education and direct service throughout the wider New Orleans community. We are also dedicated to engaging students in the conversations surrounding prison reform.

name: Tennis Club
description: Club Tennis is a coed competitive club team composed of people from all classes, and is open to graduate students as well. We typically take players who played on their varsity team in high school and competitively at USTA tournaments. In addition, we travel to out of state tournaments to places like Ole Miss, Auburn, UT Austin, and more, in which we compete against other club teams from around the country. We also do social events amongst are own team as well as with other club sports teams. This team is always a great group of people, who enjoy the time spent together on and off the court.
summary: Tulane Club Tennis

name: TAMID Group
description:
TAMID Group’s program is designed to offer experiential business learning. Beginning with an interactive classroom curriculum, the program soon progresses to give members hands-on experience with companies in the heart of the startup nation. New members participate in an interactive educational program that integrates business principles with Israel’s economic landscape. Each education semester culminates with a national case competition, where teams compete to solve a challenging business problem facing an Israeli company. After a semester of education, students may choose to join student-led consulting teams that advise Israeli companies on the solutions to important business problems. Members can also choose to join the Investment Fund program, in which teams conduct equity research and manage a stock portfolio. Each summer, members have the opportunity to spend 8-weeks in Israel on our capstone internship experience. They live and work in Tel Aviv or Jerusalem, with opportunities to learn about and explore the entire country.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: TAMID Group’s program is designed to offer experiential business learning. Beginning with an interactive classroom curriculum, the program soon progresses to give members hands-on experience with companies in the heart of the startup nation: Israel.

name: Roger Thayer Stone Center for Latin American Studies
description: * The Center comprises a diverse group of scholars representing many disciplines and points of view drawn together by our common interest in Latin America. * The Center’s principal purpose is to promote, sustain, and encourage research and teaching of Latin American Studies primarily at Tulane University. * The Center for Latin American Studies upholds a very broad definition of Latin America including geographic locations, disciplines, political and ideological perspectives. * The Center fosters a community of scholars representing a broad spectrum of ideas, interests, disciplines. * The Center has done much to encourage an interdisciplinary approach to Latin American Studies, and provides a model of a true interdisciplinarian spirit. * The Center is an integral part of the International Latin American Studies community. * The Center educates the university community about the multicultural and multi-faceted importance of Latin America and makes this importance felt in the community at large. * The Center initiates or encourages new projects involving Latin America. * The Center coordinates the training of students in Latin American Studies.
summary: * The Center comprises a diverse group of scholars representing many disciplines and points of view drawn together by our common interest in Latin America.* The Center’s principal purpose is to promote, sustain, and encourage research and teaching of L...

description: Tulane Music Students have access to the department's Computer Music Lab and Composition and Recording Studio.  In the Computer Music Lab students work with Digital Audio Workstations outfitted with software for composition, arranging, notating, and researching music and sound.  The space also serves as a workshop for students designing custom musical instruments.  The Composition and Recording Studio offers students high fidelity stereo and surround sound playback capabilities and a wide array of synthesizers, midi controllers, and professional level plugins for midi sequencing, recording, and composition.  In 2019 the department will open a newly renovated, state of the art facility for Composition and Recording projects.

description: Members of the Student Hearing Panel are individuals who are able to impartially consider evidence in cases involving misconduct within campus student organizations, make rationalized determinations regarding an organization's responsibility, and determine appropriate and educational sanctions for an organization when necessary. Serving on this panel requires a 2-year commitment, which will include a substantial time commitment for regularly scheduled trainings, meetings and hearings.

Our fraternity exists to promote the existence and welfare of the college and university bands, to honor outstanding band members, to stimulate campus leadership and promulgate an uncompromising respect through the medium of the college band, to foster a close relationship between college bands, to provide a pleasant and helpful social experience for all engaged in college band work and to cooperate with other musical organizations in any manner consistent with the purposes of Tulane.

name: Goldring Institute for International Business
description:
The Goldring Institute of International Business was founded in 1991 to house the research and academic programs of the A.B. Freeman School of Business which were related to international business. Its role is derived from the school's mission to have "a strong emphasis on the global issues facing modern business." The specific objectives of the Goldring Institute have evolved to be twofold:

provide the Freeman School of Business students the opportunity to learn about the cultural and business challenges and opportunities of the development of the global economy in which they will live and work, and;
to be a leader in management education in the developing regions of the world through the establishment of academic institutions in these regions and the export of management educational programs.
The programs offered for the Freeman School of Business students include: semester study abroad, summer abroad courses, student exchanges, the Global MBA (Master of Global Management Dual Degree Option), the MBA Global Leadership Module, and a proposed undergraduate major and minor in international business. Our MBA, EMBA, and MGM programs all require travel and classes abroad.

The programs supporting our leadership role in the developing regions include: the faculty development PhD program for Latin American faculty, the Master of Management, Master of Global Management, and International EMBA degree programs with selected schools in Eastern Europe, Latin America, and Asia.
summary: The Goldring Institute of International Business was founded in 1991 to house the research and academic programs of the A.B. Freeman School of Business which were related to international business. Its role is derived from the school's mission to have ...

name: NTC Office of Study Abroad
description:
Tulane’s NTC Office of Study Abroad facilitates the pursuit of academic exchange and study abroad for the Tulane undergraduate community. Through the close curation of a list of international programs that meet the academic and personal needs of Tulane students and one-on-one and group advising, the OSA sends approximately 1000 Tulane students abroad each year.

The Office of Study Abroad pursues the mission of assisting students achieve their academic, personal and professional goals while abroad. We believe that assisting students in the articulation of their personal and academic goals for study abroad and the pursuit of these goals will help them acquire region and country-specific knowledge, empathize with differing ways of being in the world, and apply these newfound skills in their future endeavors.

Our staff members' combined experiences studying, working and traveling abroad help us to provide instructive and meaningful assistance as they partake in their international endeavors.
summary: Tulane’s NTC Office of Study Abroad facilitates the pursuit of academic exchange and study abroad for the Tulane undergraduate community through the curation of a list of international programs that meet the academic and personal needs of Tulane students

description: Newcomb-Tulane College is the home of the undergraduate academic experience. As the undergraduate degree-granting body of Tulane University, we provide a robust core curriculum, faculty/student interaction and opportunities for academic enrichment. The various units within the college guide our students along their individual path to academic success, providing a foundation and support as well as ways to elevate their experience.

name: Tulane Chinese Christian Fellowship
description:
TCCF is a faith community of Chinese-speaking Christians in the Tulane University community, primarily graduate students, who desire to know, love, and obey God better and to reflect the love and presence of Jesus Christ. We strive to accomplish this in the following ways: a. Corporately and individually witness to Lord Jesus Christ as God incarnated and seek to lead Chinese-speaking students, faculty and staff to personal faith in Christ as Lord and Savior. b. Help Chinese-speaking Christians grow toward maturity as Disciples of Christ, by study of the Bible, by prayer, by worship, and by Christian Fellowship. c. Help Chinese-speaking students, faculty and staff to discover God’s role for them. d. Help meet the physical and personal needs of Chinese-speaking students and scholars at Tulane University and in the New Orleans Area.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: CPS Internship Program
description: The Tulane Center for Public Service Internship Program provides students with a competitive advantage by offering the opportunity to build skills and abilities through intensive community-based work. The Internship Program acts a three credit second-tier public service course. To earn elective and public service credit through this program, students complete an internship and participate in an internship seminar.
summary: Public Service Internships provide students the opportunity to build skills and abilities through intensive community-based work. Student earn 3 credit hours and second-tier public service credit by completing an internship and seminar course.

name: Tulane Sports Analytics Club
description:
The purpose of the club is to unite undergraduate and graduate students in research into sports analytics. One goal is to help students interested in a career in sports enhance their skills, resumes, and portfolios. Whether pursuing a career in sports or not, we want all students to learn more about sports, statistics, and data.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The purpose of the club is to unite undergraduate and graduate students in education and research into sports analytics to help students interested in a career in sports enhance their skills, resumes, and portfolios.

name: Office of Fraternity and Sorority Programs
description: Fraternity and Sorority Programs is committed to fostering a positive co-curricular experience for students who choose to affiliate with a Greek organization. Through its various programs, the department is committed to supporting the valuable learning experience that the Greek community offers.
summary: Fraternity and Sorority Programs is committed to fostering a positive co-curricular experience for students who choose to affiliate with a Greek organization through various programs and development opportunities.

Tulane AIGA is established for the purpose of bringing together students who either participate in design-related fields or appreciate or advocate for design and design thinking. The secondary purpose of Tulane AIGA is to establish an extension of the local chapter of AIGA New Orleans and connect with local and national professionals to help further design graduates in their professional and emerging designer goals.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Tulane AIGA is established for the purpose of bringing together students who either participate in design-related fields or appreciate or advocate for design and design thinking. The secondary purpose of Tulane AIGA is to establish an extension of the...

name: Campus Recreation
description: The Department of Campus Recreation welcomes you to participate in our diverse wellness and recreation activities!  Students can find late night activities, unique fitness opportunities, outdoor adventures, and a lot of fun and excitement at TU Rec. Programs include Intramural Sports, Club Sports, Fitness and Wellness, Instructional Programs, and Outdoor Adventures. Recreation space includes the 156,000 square foot Reily Student Recreation Center with a 5,000sq ft weight room, a functional fitness gym, two outdoor tennis courts, and a synthetic turf field. Campus Recreation is the largest student employer on campus with approximately 300 student employees.
summary: The Department of Campus Recreation offers some of the most popular and participated in programs and services on campus. Programs include Intramural Sports, Club Sports, Fitness and Wellness, Instructional Programs, Aquatics, and Outdoor Recreation.

summary: Office of the President

name: Student Employment
description: The Student Employment team is committed to assisting students through the entire hiring process and throughout their employment. We work in collaboration with Tulane's Financial Aid, Office of International Students and Scholars, and Student Affairs to provide student workers with a positive work experience.
summary: The Student Employment team is committed to assisting students through the entire hiring process and throughout their employment.

name: Delta Delta Delta
description: Tri Delta is an assembly of women with shared values where you can be yourself and belong to something bigger. We are brave, bold and kind. We are passionate about serving others, committed to becoming the best version of ourselves and dedicated to helping women live, learn and lead – with Purpose – for a lifetime.
summary: Tri Delta is a sorority that fosters perpetual bonds of friendships, promises lifelong sisterhood, & a network of support. Through our innovative education & leadership programs, we provide members with personal, professional & leadership development.

name: Tulane Public Service Advisory Board
description: The Tulane Public Service Advisory Board is a group of selected undergraduate students dedicated to re-inventing Public Service at Tulane. We work alongside the Center for Public Service to find the weakness that we have in our community and come together to find ways we can improve. We are driven to improve the quality and impact public service has on Tulane students.
summary: The Tulane Public Service Advisory Board is a group of selected undergraduate students dedicated to re-inventing pubilc service at Tulane.

name: Office of Sustainability
description: The Office of Sustainability collaborates with students, staff, faculty, and community partners to reduce the university’s environmental impact and promote a culture of sustainability.
summary: The Office of Sustainability partners with students, staff and faculty to make a more sustainable university.

name: Rudolph Matas Library of the Health Sciences
summary: Rudolph Matas Library of the Health Sciences is located on the 2nd floor of the Hutchinson Building at 1430 Tulane Avenue in New Orleans, LA.

name: Tulane Cancer Center
summary: Tulane Cancer Center

name: Innovative Learning Center
description: The mission of the Innovative Learning Center (ILC) is to promote the strategic advancement of Tulane’s academic mission through the incorporation and development of academic technology across all aspects of university life.
summary: The ILC serves as a focal point for faculty interested in exploring new tactics for delivering their lessons and assessing their students.  The Center staff also consult on research strategies and act as liaisons between IT and faculty.

name: NTC Marketing and Communications
description: Marketing and Communications manages the brand, marketing and events for Newcomb-Tulane College.
summary: The NTC Marketing and Communications Department promotes meaningful events to undergraduate students.

name: Semester Journalism Experience
description: Learn how to be a journalist and multimedia storyteller while exploring the role of media in our society. Participants will meet with professional journalists and practice hands on skills while completing a semester-long project and being mentored by upper class members of Tulane student media. No prior experience necessary and the program is free!
summary: Learn how to be a journalist and multimedia storyteller while exploring the role of media in our society.

name: The Foundation for the International Medical Relief of Children
description:
FIMRC is a nonprofit dedicated to improving healthcare for families in underdeveloped countries throughout the world. Through emphasizing education as a way of taking preventative measures, FIMRC hopes to provide more sustainable healthcare to families in need. This program is supported by a number of volunteers that travel to FIMRC's health clinic locations throughout various countries, including Ecuador, Peru, the Philippines, and Costa Rica. These clinics offer free care for local families who are unable to afford it. Abroad, volunteers can make a direct impact working in these clinics with patients by taking vitals and distributing medications. Locally, volunteers organize various donation drives for medical supplies that are given to the health clinics; these supplies are then distributed to the local families in need. Local volunteers also participate in a number of clinical volunteering events including hospital visits and often spend time working to spread ideas of FIMRC's mission regarding more sustainable healthcare through education. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: FIMRC is a nonprofit organization that is dedicated to improving healthcare for families in underdeveloped countries. The organization focuses on providing quality care while also focusing on the importance of education.

name: CHAARG
description:
We meet weekly as a large group to try out a new workout, exploring different fitness studios around New Orleans. Most of our sessions will take place off campus, but we’ll also occasionally invite instructors to Tulane for on-campus classes. In addition to our weekly workouts, we’ll meet in small groups to support one another in living balanced, healthy, and fulfilling lives as young women and college students.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: CHAARG is a health and wellness group that stands for changing health attitudes and actions to recreate girls.

name: Tulane University Fashion Club
description:
Welcome to Tulane University's Fashion club! We provide a creative outlet for those passionate about expressing themselves and empowering others through style, design, and individuality. Alongside hosting a premier annual fashion show and publishing a graphic style print, we host speakers from the fashion industry, offer skills workshops to learn, explore, and create fashion, and promote the networking of Tulane students with the New Orleans fashion community. Anyone with an interest in fashion, designing, merchandising, marketing, fashion business, event planning, styling or creative expression is encouraged to join! 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Welcome to Tulane University's Fashion club! We provide a creative outlet for those passionate about expressing themselves and empowering others through style, design, and individuality.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description:
Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers is a community passionate about advocating for hispanic representation in STEM fields. We connect members with networking, professional opportunities, and lasting friendships!

Open to all majors & backgrounds!

Mission: SHPE changes lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development.

Vision: SHPE’s vision is a world where Hispanics are highly valued and influential as the leading innovators, scientists, mathematicians and engineers.

Values: Familia, Service, Education, Resilience
summary:
Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers is a community passionate about advocating for hispanic representation in STEM fields. We connect members with networking, professional opportunities, and lasting friendships!
Open to all majors & backgrounds!

name: Black Queer Collective
description:
The Black Queer Collective will be a society centering on the experiences of Black students who are members of the LGBTQ+ community. The society is dedicated to providing a space for Black Queer folk to meet, share ideas and concerns, raise concerns, raise awareness of issues affecting both the Black and/or queer communities. The goal is to create a sense of camaraderie.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: BQC will center the experiences of Black students who are in the LGBTQ+ community. It is dedicated to providing a space for Black Queer folk to meet, share ideas and concerns, raise awareness of issues affecting both the Black and/or queer communities.

Meet new people through a wide assortment of fun party games including classics such as:

- Halo
