name: Powerlifting Club
description: Tulane Powerlifting’s purpose is to improve the health and fitness of Tulane students through strength training while also providing the opportunity to compete against other universities at Collegiate Nationals in the sport of powerlifting.
summary: Tulane Powerlifting’s purpose is to improve the health and fitness of Tulane students through strength training while also providing the opportunity to compete against other universities at Collegiate Nationals in the sport of powerlifting.

name: New Orleans Center for the Gulf South at Tulane University
description: The New Orleans Center for the Gulf South at Tulane University (NOCGS) is an interdisciplinary, place-based institute that was founded in 2011 and is now a cornerstone of the School of Liberal Arts. NOCGS is dedicated to preserving, perpetuating, and celebrating the distinctive cultures of New Orleans and the Gulf South, identified as the bioregion stretching from Florida to Texas.  The Center’s mission rests on three pillars: research, teaching, and community engagement. All of our programming is based on the belief that the more we understand where we are, the more fully we can engage in our democracy and collective destiny.
summary: The New Orleans Center for the Gulf South at Tulane University (NOCGS) is an interdisciplinary, place-based institute that was founded in 2011 and is now a cornerstone of the School of Liberal Arts.

name: Newcomb Art Department
description:
The Newcomb Art Department is devoted to the study and production of visual art within the context of Tulane University's strong tradition in the liberal arts. It fosters an environment of independent and collective exploration that promotes original thought, creative inquiry, and visual literacy. 

.

The studio art program develops students’ skills of rendering and fabrication as well as perception, composition and execution. Exposure to historical and contemporary art, vigorous analysis and comparative examination provide substantial grounding of practice in theory.

The art history program educates students in the visual and historical analysis of art from antiquity to the present. Its faculty exposes students to an array of methodological approaches to interpret art across different regions and periods, fostering rigorous thinking and effective verbal expression.

The department enriches the university and community, serving as an advocate for the visual arts by presenting exhibitions, lectures, and other programs. The department contributes to the creation and analysis of visual art by supporting original scholarship and creativity among its faculty on regional, national, and international levels.
summary: The Newcomb Art Department is devoted to the study and production of visual art within the context of Tulane University's strong tradition in the liberal arts.

name: Mellon Graduate Program in Community-Engaged Scholarship
description:
The program was launched in 2017 in conjunction with a grant from the Andrew W. Mellon Foundation, following three years of pilot graduate programs in community engagement at Tulane. Through the “Transforming Graduate Education through Engaging the Community” grant, Tulane is building on its strength as a campus rich in community-engaged faculty from a wide range of departments as well as its strength as an institution with over six hundred established community partnerships in New Orleans and globally.

The Tulane Mellon Graduate Program supports graduate students doing public humanities work and aims to:

  • build a public humanities program that gradually persuades skeptics about the capacities for such work to add new dimensions to graduate student education and enrich their home disciplines;
  • bring cultural and community leaders from outside academia into the core of a graduate program and value diverse knowers and ways of knowing;
  • be aware of and work against power structures of various sorts that can inhibit healthy relationships and genuine collaboration;
  • work with graduate students to help them develop public humanities projects that connect with the graduate students’ studies from their home departments, making the work more sustainable over the course of a career;
  • facilitate the development of new skills and experiences through substantial collaborative projects for the public good, complete with budgets, MOU’s, and evaluation systems;
  • design a graduate program in public humanities that helps to build more substantial networks of universities, artists, government, as well as cultural and community organizations throughout New Orleans and the gulf south.
summary: The Tulane Mellon Graduate Program in Community-Engaged Scholarship is an initiative of Tulane’s Office of the Provost and School of Liberal Arts.

name: Women Investing in Women
description:
The Tulane Women Investing in Women is an organization designed to promote the goals and needs of students interested or majoring in Finance. The organization provides information regarding careers related to Finance, opportunities to connect and learn from women in the financial industry, and connecting with peers with similar career goals for undergraduate business students. Participation or membership in Women Investing in Women is open to all students, regardless of their gender. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Tulane Women Investing in Women is an organization designed to promote the goals and needs of students interested or majoring in Finance.

name: Tulane University Campus Cats
description:
TUCC is an organization that is designed to protect the campus cat population from actions taken by administration, to educate students and faculty about the cats and how to keep them safe, to fundraise to provide resources to help feed and care for the cats when they are ill, and to provide students a way to interact with the campus cats. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: TUCC is an organization established to bring students, faculty, and staff alike together in order to protect and maintain the Tulane campus cat population. The organization will also educate Tulane University on the campus cats and their roles at Tulane.

name: Career Wave Programs
description: Career Wave serves as an intersection between students and alumni/parents for engagement in career related education, networking and career exploration. Career Wave plans and executes events specifically for students to interact with professionals.
summary: Career Wave serves as an intersection between students and alumni/parents for engagement in career related education, networking and career exploration. Career Wave plans and executes events specifically for students to interact with professionals.

name: Alliance of Black Business Students
description:
The Alliance of Black Business Students (ABBS) is a coalition of students enrolled in the Freeman Business School. We are devoted to promoting diversity within the Freeman Business School as well as providing support and networking opportunities. Participation in the ABBS programs, activities, and events is open to all students, regardless of race.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: ABBS is a coalition of students devoted to promoting diversity within the Freeman Business School as well as providing support and networking opportunities. Participation in ABBS is open to all students, regardless of race.

name: The Tulane Journal of Policy and Political Economy
description:
The Tulane Journal of Policy and Political Economy, an internationally recognized student-run publication, provides a platform for undergraduate academic writing at both Tulane University and the greater U.S. undergraduate academic population overall. Funded by Newcomb-Tulane College and in collaboration with Tulane’s Departments of Political Science and Economics, the Tulane School of Public Health, and student societies at Oxford and the London School of Economics, the Journal concentrates on the intersection of policy, politics, and economic theory. The Journal spans a range of multimedia platforms, including physical and digital distribution of the semi-annual journal, regular podcast releases, and academic events such as a speaker series and writing workshops. Its inaugural edition was, moreover, indexed in the Library of Congress.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Tulane Journal of Policy and Political Economy is an internationally recognized academic publication run exclusively by Tulane undergrads. The Journal publishes 2 annual editions and hosts by-weekly virtual events.

name: Pinky Swear Pack Tulane
description:
The Pinky Swear Pack is derived from the national non- profit organization "The Pinky Swear Foundation." The Pinky Swear Foundation has created a national network of "Pinky Swear Packs," which consist of college students passionate about helping children fighting cancer. Pack Members have the opportunity to visit children in hospitals, support facilities, and host fun adventures to create therapeutic experiences that have an incredible impact on the lives of children during and after treatment. Consisting of a maximum of 30 member per academic semester, packs are sent fundraising supplies and more from the national organization. Pack members talk to the kids and their families, do arts and crafts, and play games on hospital visits. Currently, the Pinky Swear Foundation has officially recognized the Tulane Pinky Swear Pack as one of many college packs around the country. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Pinky Swear PACK is a nationally recognized organization with a mission to enrich the lives of kids battling cancer. It has "campus crews" across the country who work to give kids enriching experiences through hospital visits and fun activities.

name: Department of Chemistry
summary: Tulane's Department of Chemistry has been devoted to the high quality teaching and research since its founding in 1834.

name: LBC Student Employees
summary: Homepage for student employees at the Tulane University Lavin-Bernick Center for University life to access resources, submit required forms, and connect with professional staff.

name: Department of Tropical Medicine and Infectious Disease
description:
The Department of Tropical Medicine (TropMed) has a long and prestigious history as one of the oldest institutions studying, preventing and managing tropical diseases.  A leader in the field, Tulane TropMed has a strong international reputation for research and education in vector-borne and other tropical infectious diseases. TropMed addresses tropical diseases from the clinical, laboratory and epidemiological approaches.

The degree programs educate students in field work, epidemiology and evidence-based prevention and control, along with state-of-the-art laboratory studies of tropical diseases. Graduates are prepared to work in disease control programs, diagnostic parasitology labs, and academic and research laboratories. 

Department faculty have extensive expertise in areas such as mosquito biology and biochemistry, medical and biochemical entomology, tropical virology, and population-based prevention and evaluation. They conduct basic and applied research on vector-borne infectious diseases like Chagas, Dengue fever, West Nile virus, Lassa fever, and Ebola virus.
summary: Tropical Medicine conducts research in tropical medicine, parasitology and applied population-based malaria evaluation and educates students to address the clinical, laboratory and evidence-based approaches to prevent and control tropical diseases.

name: Office of Medical Education
summary: This department oversees the Medical educational activities, curriculum and operations.

name: Jewish Studies
description:
We are already nationally recognized, standing well above our peers in many key metrics. But we are striving for even greater heights. The cornerstone of our strategy to move to an elite level is the Stuart and Suzanne Grant Center for the American Jewish Experience, through which we will become the academic leader for the innovative and holistic study of American Jewry. Creating new intellectual connections, the Grant Center will transform the field by practicing across disciplinary and subfield boundaries, emphasizing the global orientation of the American Jewish experience.

We will build a world class faculty and implement a dynamic and innovative slate of programming, pioneering new areas of inquiry in the study of American Jewry. Taken together, this will position Tulane as a hub of excellence and the global leader in the study of the American Jewish experience.
summary: Our vision for Tulane's Department of Jewish Studies will build upon the strengths of the university and create a world-class hub of Jewish learning.

name: Healthcare Policy Club
description:
The Healthcare Policy Club is dedicated to exploring the different facets of healthcare policy and its impact on individuals, communities, and the future of healthcare. This organization promotes learning about healthcare, public policy, or social justice, and membership is open to all students. Through group discussions and guest speakers, our members gain valuable knowledge about healthcare policy, post-graduate trajectories, and career opportunities while connecting with fellow students, current Tulane faculty members, and professionals in the field. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Healthcare Policy Club welcomes all interested students to explore healthcare policy's impact, learn about related careers through discussions and speaker events, and connect with peers, professors, and professionals.

name: Strong City NOLA at Tulane University
shortName: Strong City's Tulane Chapter, Strong City NOLA, Strong City Tulane
description:
Strong City NOLA is a Tulane Chapter of Strong City, a 501(c)(3). Strong City was founded by Dr. Peter Gold, a Tulane Undergrad and Medical School alumni, who in 2015 was shot in the stomach while trying to stop a woman from being abducted on the streets of New Orleans. The question that haunted Peter the most about this chaotic, violent event was, “what happened in this young person’s life where their first instinct was shoot to kill?” After recovering, Peter started Strong City with his classmates from Tulane in order to make a difference in the lives of young people in New Orleans who are affected by poverty and violence and disrupt the cycles that facilitate these issues.

Strong City does this through project based giving, gun violence prevention research, and a network of Strong Citizens. We are currently partnered with the Youth Empowerment Project (YEP), the New Orleans Women's and Children's Shelter (NOWCS), the Community Academies of New Orleans (CANO) and more. Strong City supports our partner organizations through fundraisers, volunteer support, events hosted at their facilities, and our own Strong City-hosted events. 

As a member of Strong City Tulane (founded in 2019) you will join us in our mission to help the youth of New Orleans. We have monthly fun members meetings, plentiful volunteer opportunities on and off campus, and many ways to take on leadership and get further involved. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Strong City 501(c)(3) is Tulane University's largest student-run non-profit organization dedicated to disrupting cycles of poverty and violence in New Orleans through collaborative efforts with youth-focused organizations.

name: Hearts for the Homeless New Orleans
description:
• Hearts for the Homeless New Orleans is a non-profit organization that serves to provide one of the most vulnerable populations with free blood pressure screenings, education on the causes of high blood pressure, and clinical outreach programs. Through partnering with local food drives and community health clinics, H4H strives to reduce the prevalence of hypertension in the unhoused community of New Orleans. Furthermore, Hearts for the Homeless seeks to educate its members about cardiovascular disease, patient interaction, and hidden difficulties in the unhoused community through volunteer experience. This organization assists with shaping the minds of our members into compassionate leaders along with spreading awareness of cardiovascular disease to underprivileged communities in New Orleans.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Hearts for the Homeless New Orleans is a non-profit organization that serves to provide one of the most vulnerable populations with free blood pressure screenings, education on the causes of high blood pressure, and clinical outreach programs.

name: Brazilian Student Association
description:
BRASA is an organization that is present in over 60 University Campuses in the United States, and has over 14,000 members globally. The organization offers networking and professional opportunities to its members. BRASA is affiliated with some of the biggest Brazilian companies. The organization is not limited to Brazilian students; we are open to anyone interested in Brazilian culture and/or working in Brazil. We plan on promoting cultural activities such as Portuguese Workshops (free Portuguese classes) and Conversation Nights (informal meetings to practice Portuguese). BRASA Tulane members will have access to BRASA Careers, an online portal in which prestigious companies post job and internship offerings specifically to members of the BRASA community. Some of the organizations that recruit BRASA students/alumni are Ambev, Redbull, Unilever, JP Morgan, United Nations, and many others.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Our mission is to strengthen the Brazilian community at Tulane and to engage students interested in Brazil's culture, language and traditions. In addition BRASA provides networking and internship/job opportunities to its community.

organizationType.name: Recognized Student Organizations - Multicultural, Religious & GSAC

name: Tulane Surf Club
description:
The Tulane Surf Club will support students who are passionate about water/board sports and the health and safety of the world’s beaches, oceans, and bodies of water. Its purpose is to:

-Take students on weekend surf trips. The closest location to surf is Grand Isle, roughly a two-hour drive away.

-Perform cleanup activities at beaches, riverfronts, and lakefronts.

-Support organizations such as the Surfrider foundation, an organization dedicated to the protection of the world's oceans, waves, and beaches through fundraising events.

-Teach students beginner to advanced watersporting skills. 

-Provide a platform for Tulane students to share their passion of the ocean and water sports.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Surf Club is for students interested in getting involved with the New Orleans surfing community and marine conservation. Accepts all levels of surfers, newcomers and advanced. Partners with non-profits doing sustainability projects across the Gulf South

name: TEDxTulane
description:
TEDxTulane is engaged in the business of public speaking and idea-sharing. Our goal is to provide a platform for those motivational speakers, professional life coaches, and ambitious Tulane students who otherwise would not have gotten the chance to share their stories or discoveries. Through this collaboration, ideas about technology, science, social health, business, and more can be presented on a global stage. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: TEDxTulane is a student ran organization focused on bringing together people of all different fields in order to share innovative ideas and inspiring stories.

name: Tulane Fintech
description:
Tulane Fintech, or T.U.F.T , is a student organization on campus promoting financial technology and educating the student body about financial technology, trending technology, and blockchain-based services including blockchain, business, and cryptocurrency. We explore the intricacies of emerging technologies and blockchain-based software, services, and tools. Tulane Fintech has many events throughout the year including but not limited to: opportunities to go to some of the top Fintech Conferences around the country (Bitcoin 2024 in Nashville), participating in hackathons, speaker series with renowned members of the industry such as the CEO of Circle, Senate advisors on fintech policy, knowledgable professors, and top venture capital partners. In joining Tulane Fintech, one is also joining the Crypto Current Network, our national Financial Literacy platform. Crypto Current Network gives students unique economic and networking opportunities. Moreover, we help our students with the internship and job recruitment process for Financial Technology related roles. 

Our mission is simple: Educate Future Leaders on New Financial Opportunities

Join our GroupMe for updates and opportunities: https://groupme.com/join_group/91909545/19uWl23B

Tulane Fintech is the first chapter under the parental national organization, Crypto Current Network. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Tulane Fintech, or TU Fintech, is a student organization on campus promoting financial technology and educating the student body about financial technology, trending tech, and blockchain-based services including blockchain, business, and cryptocurrency.

name: Tulane Disability and Access Coalition
description:
The Tulane Disability and Access Coalition is an alliance of students, professors, and campus organizations to promote the equal participation and opportunity of individuals with disabilities in all aspects of life. We operate to advocate for public policy that ensures autonomy, empowerment, and full inclusion for people with disabilities in areas such as healthcare, education, accessibility, housing, motor access, and civil rights. The work of self-advocates and allies is critical to the success of our organization. We strive to make our campus more accessible. We work together and brainstorm creative ideas that support individuals with disabilities, while also creating bonds, learning from each other's experiences. Our goal is to create a "safe space" of solidarity on Tulane's campus for all students, including students with disabilities.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Tulane's Disability & Access Coalition is an alliance of students, professors, and organizers. Our goals are to promote equity for those with disabilities & foster connections with each other. We discuss our experiences and how to make campus accessible.

name: Department of Health Policy & Management
description: This department prepares students to meet the ever-changing demands of the health care, public health, and health policy environments using financial models, organizational structures, and social determinants to improve quality, contain costs, improve access to health care, and address health disparities for all populations. We envision a future where our graduates enhance quality of life by improving public health and healthcare delivery systems worldwide.
summary: Our department prepares a diverse group of future managers and leaders to improve health care delivery services, increase access to health care, reduce health disparities, and advance health care policy worldwide.

name: Office of Academic Affairs
summary: This office oversees the appointment, advancement, and retention of faculty; the articulation and deployment of all academic and research programs; the development of strategic plans to secure the University’s enduring excellence in all endeavors.

name: Newcomb-Tulane College First-Year Experience
description:
The First-Year Experience begins when you choose Tulane as your academic home.

By saying ‘yes’ to Tulane, you can access a wealth of dynamic experiences that connect you with your classmates, introduce you to your faculty, and ground you in your new home. During your first year, you will be welcomed into Tulane’s intellectual community by engaging in academic discussion with faculty and peers. You will learn of the many resources you can refer to for support and engagement. You will grow your social circle as you discover your community of people.

There is much to look forward to during your first year at Tulane. We are so glad you’re here.
summary: Newcomb-Tulane College's First Year Experience connects students with classmates, faculty, and success resources throughout your first year at Tulane. Including programs like First-Year Seminars, The Reading Project, New Student Orientation, and more.

name: Tulane Synthetic Biology Club
description:
The Tulane Synthetic Biology Club (TU Synbio) is a group of synthetic biology enthusiasts who work on a team research project and compete in synthetic biology competitions such as the Global Open Genetic Engineering Competition (Gogec).

Please email Laney Druhan (kdruhan@tulane.edu) if you are interested in joining our Slack!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: Tulane University Roller Skating Club
description:
TURSC is established for the purpose of bringing together Tulane students who share a common appreciation of roller skating and/or blading. The goal of TURSC is to (1) provide a network where students can connect with other fellow roller skaters and/or bladers and to (2) provide regular group meet-ups.

Membership in TURSC is open to all Tulane University affiliates. All skill levels are welcome to join this club, and free loaner skates and blades are available for the regular group skates. 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

The Tulane Chess Club aims to facilitate the love and sense of community revolving around the game of chess on Tulane's campus! We host events such as tournaments, beginners' workshops, simuls, and even interviews and games with notable chess personalities (Hikaru Nakamura, Vladimir Kramnik, Jude Acers, etc.). We meet every Wednesday from 4-6 p.m. in the LBC unless otherwise planned - join our GroupMe for specifics! We hope to see you at any one of our events!

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: MEDLIFE
description:
MEDLIFE's mission is to build a worldwide Movement empowering the poor in their fight for equal access to healthcare, education, and a safe home. MEDLIFE believes that in order to improve the overall health and welfare of families and communities in need, a comprehensive approach is needed. With this goal in mind, MEDLIFE seeks to deliver aid through three broad avenues: Medicine, Education, and Development. When patients are simply referred to local hospitals for treatments many times they still fail to receive the actual treatment they need due to a lack of resources, lack of confidence, or lack of a true understanding of their medical problems. For all of these reasons, MEDLIFE has developed a year-round patient follow-up process. We support our patients in many ways. We provide financial resources for treatments, emotional support, nutritional assistance for malnourished families, and educational resources to help individuals better understand their conditions. Students also have the opportunity to participate in a Service Learning Trip, where they help set up mobile clinics in low-income villages and shadow local healthcare professionals as well as complete a larger collaborative service project for the community.

To help fund this movement, feel free to donate to our Tulane University MEDLife page:  https://secure.givelively.org/teams/medlife/moving-mountains-ignite-the-light/tulane-university

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: MEDLIFE's mission is to build a worldwide Movement empowering the poor in their fight for equal access to healthcare, education, and a safe home. MEDLIFE seeks to deliver aid through three broad avenues: Medicine, Education, and Development.

name: Office of Academic Excellence & Opportunity
description: The pursuit of academic excellence and opportunity fuels Tulane University’s continued progress. The hallmark of a great university is its ability to build a community of scholars and students from a wide variety of disciplines, backgrounds, and experiences who converge to ignite discovery, advance scholarship, and experience transformative personal growth. This is the foundation of Tulane University’s academic excellence and opportunity, as well as the source of our positive impact on society. At the very center of our mission is a culture personified by every Tulanian— a compassionate, close-knit place for students, faculty, staff, alumni, and friends. Our success depends upon our ability to grow and strengthen this community by attracting individuals from all backgrounds and experiences and providing each Tulanian with the opportunity to pursue their goals and aspirations and reach their fullest potential. In embracing these principles, we not only advance our mission as a leading research university but also contribute to a world where every individual can thrive.
summary: The Office of Academic Excellence & Opportunity building a Tulane where we practice our core value of welcoming community every day.

Priorities for the office include fostering interdisciplinary collaboration, aiding in the recruitment and retention of leading investigators, and building the research portfolio in a manner compliant with government regulation.  The Office of Research includes eight units:  Biosafety, Comparative Medicine, Human Research Protection (IRB), Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC), Research Proposal Development, Sponsored Projects Administration, Research Compliance, and Technology Transfer and Intellectual Property Development.

name: A Studio in the Woods
description:
A Studio in the Woods, a program of Tulane University’s ByWater Institute, fosters creative responses to the challenges of our time by providing retreat to artists, scholars, and the public in our protected forest on the Mississippi River.

 

The Studio focuses on interrelated areas of programming, including residencies for artists and scholars, forest restoration, and science-inspired art engagement for children and adults. The Studio’s work is based on the premise that southern Louisiana is a microcosm of the global environment, manifesting both the challenges and possibilities inherent in human interaction with urban and natural ecosystems. We center inter-dependence, wonder, care and inclusivity in all of our work and believe the essential and transformative powers of art and the natural environment are central to a thriving future for all.

 

Learn more at www.astudiointhewoods.org
summary: A Studio in the Woods, a program of Tulane University’s ByWater Institute, fosters creative responses to the challenges of our time by providing retreat to artists, scholars, and the public in our protected forest on the Mississippi River.

As the largest undergraduate college at Tulane University, the School of Liberal Arts encompasses the humanities, fine & performing arts, and social sciences on campus. Our events include annual Dean's Office priorities including our New Student Fall Open House, Dean's Speaker Series, Bobby Yan Lectureship in Media & Social Change, Carole Barnette Boudreaux '65 Great Writers Series, Catch Up with Dean Edwards, as well as daily programming ranging from talks and workshops to networking events, from 16 departments & 19 programs.

For an overview of upcoming events across our school, visit https://liberalarts.tulane.edu/news-events/events.

name: The Albert and Tina Small Center for Collaborative Design
description:
We contend that innovative solutions to the most pressing problems facing our community lie in giving voice to residents. Our partner organizations bring their project ideas to us, and we bring our design expertise to bear in collaboration, supporting New Orleans residents in imagining and pursuing projects that strengthen neighborhoods and contribute to a city shaped by its residents.

Learn More
summary: The Albert and Tina Small Center for Collaborative Design is the community design center of the Tulane School of Architecture. We are located at 1725 Baronne Street.

name: Digital Media Practices
description:
The creative industries are a cornerstone of New Orleans’ culture and economy. Digital Media Practices provide students opportunities to develop and refine digital media skills omnipresent across exhibition spaces and streaming platforms. Students pursue fundamentals through coursework in content creation, storytelling, and media manipulation in filmmaking, screenwriting, audio production, Interactive media, game design, podcasting, and interdisciplinary media. Our workshop-style classes allow experienced and supportive faculty to define student goals and actively collaborate — critical soft skills in the media production industry. 

The major culminates in a two-semester Capstone sequence, during which students develop, produce, and distribute a professional-grade media work in their chosen medium and genre. This senior project aims for students to creatively integrate storytelling with digital practices across cinematic, sound, and data-driven media. While many students choose to create entirely produced narrative short films, program faculty challenge students to formulate innovative projects of their design, including documentary short films, full-length screenplays, multi-episode podcasts, radio dramas, interactive media exhibitions, experimental video games, or a combination of similar elements. 

The Coordinate Major in Digital Media Practices requires students to declare a major in another discipline before declaring the DMPC coordinate major. The program is an interdisciplinary, 30-credit major that can include courses from the Music, Theatre and Dance, Communication, Cinema Studies, Art, and English Departments, and the Strategy, Leadership & Analytics Minor.  Declared majors have priority enrollment in classes that emphasize the use of industry-standard technologies and equipment.
summary: A coordinate major program that prepares students for careers in Screenwriting, Producing, Podcasting, Film Production and Post-Production, Production Sound, Sound Design, Interactive Media, and Game Design.

name: Albert Lepage Center for Innovation & Entrepreneurship
description: The Lepage Center offers programs and resources geared toward the entire Tulane community, including faculty, staff, students and alumni both within Freeman and across campus. In addition, we work with entrepreneurs in the Greater New Orleans and Gulf South regions.
summary: The Albert Lepage Center for Entrepreneurship and Innovation at the Freeman School of Business brings together scholars, inventors, investors and students to solve problems and build businesses.

name: Altman Program in International Studies & Business
description:
The purpose of the Jeffrey A. Altman Program in International Studies & Business is to provide a select group of undergraduate students with a unique international experience by imparting to them both theoretical and practical knowledge of global economies in tandem with deep cultural and linguistic competency training. The dual-degree program will lead to a Bachelor of Science in Management in a business discipline and a Bachelor of Arts in a liberal arts discipline. The “Altman Core”—a series of semester-long, unique, cohort-based classes—serves to provide common academic experiences to our scholars as they matriculate through this rigorous program. Altman Scholars benefit from the guidance and mentoring by Tulane’s faculty and staff (both from the Altman team proper and from other departments on campus) as they develop a thoughtful understanding of complex global issues, as well as the role that these issues play in international business environments. Other highlights of the program include intensive foreign language instruction (current choices are Spanish, French, German, Chinese, Italian, Portuguese, and Japanese), a fully-funded cohort-based Rising Sophomore program abroad, and an entire year abroad studying in their target language during their Junior year.

 

Learn more about the Altman Program in our informational video.
summary: A dual-degree undergraduate program that integrates liberal arts and business disciplines, extensive language instruction, two study abroad experiences, and intercultural awareness.

name: Tulane Global Humanities Center
description: The Tulane Global Humanities Center (TGHC) is a gathering place for Tulane scholars and students, as well as visitors and guests from the New Orleans community and beyond. It centers the humanities and amplifes scholarship and teaching that takes a global perspective broadly understood, recognizing that Tulane’s location in New Orleans/Bulbancha is at once a global port city and a key nexus in the Gulf South. Building off both of these aspects and exploring their intersections inspires the Center’s activities and trajectory. By organizing interdisciplinary biennial themes, the TGHC will generate conversations, scholarship, and teaching that underlines the importance of the humanities in addressing some of the most challenging problems of our times and opening them up to new perspectives and insights.
summary: Tulane Global Humanities Center

name: Tulane University School of Social Work
description:
Tulane University School of Social Work provides innovative, top-tier, high-touch graduate school programs to equip future leaders with clinical and community-based practice training that empowers individuals, families, and communities. For over 100 years, our students have received a transformative education steeped in engagement, research, and excellence.

Everything the School of Social Work does puts knowledge into practice to help others, no matter who or where they are. Our faculty engages in innovative research and education that is transferrable anywhere, so that students can extend what they learn into our community or across the globe to affect change from the personal to societal level. Students have a choice in how they focus their learning and engage with information. They can elect a Master of Social Work, a Doctorate of Social Work, a Master of Science in Disaster Resilience Leadership, or one of our dual degree programs with Public Health, Law, or the MSW/MS-DRL. We also offer the MSW, MSW/MS-DRL, and DSW in an accessible, quality, high-touch online learning environment.
summary: Tulane University School of Social Work provides innovative, top-tier, high-touch graduate school that equips future leaders with clinical and community-based practice training that empowers individuals, families, and communities.

name: Judeo-Christian Studies
description: The Catherine and Henry J. Gaisman Chair of Judeo-Christian Studies directs a series of public lectures and seminars with a focus on fundamental religious, moral, and philosophic issues. The program, founded by Father Val McInnes and Prof. Frank Birtel, now directed by Prof. Ronna Burger, has brought prominent scholars to campus since 1980.  Our lectures, which usually take place in Rogers Memorial Chapel, bring together faculty, students, and members of the community.  Seven volumes of collected essays have been published and videos are available of events since 2011.  The Gaisman Chair also provides support for Tulane’s Religious Studies Minor program.  For further information, see our website: https://liberalarts.tulane.edu/judeo-christian-studies.
summary: Judeo-Christian Studies oversees a series of lectures and seminars on themes related to the Judeo-Christian tradition, with a focus on important religious, moral, and philosophic issues, as well as supporting the Religious Studies Minor.

name: Cell and Molecular Biology
description:
Tulane University's Cell and Molecular Biology Department is a leading academic department focused on studying the fundamental principles of life at the cellular and molecular levels. The department's research and educational programs are designed to explore diverse areas such as cell structure and function, molecular genetics, developmental biology, neurobiology, and biotechnology.

Faculty members in the Cell and Molecular Biology Department are actively engaged in cutting-edge research, often collaborating across disciplines to address complex biological questions. Students in the department have access to state-of-the-art laboratories and facilities, as well as opportunities to participate in research projects alongside faculty members.

The department offers undergraduate and graduate programs, providing students with a comprehensive understanding of cellular and molecular processes, as well as hands-on research experience. Through coursework, seminars, and research opportunities, students gain the knowledge and skills necessary for careers in academia, industry, healthcare, and other scientific fields.

Overall, Tulane University's Cell and Molecular Biology Department is dedicated to advancing scientific knowledge and training the next generation of researchers and professionals in the field.
summary: We are a department made up of faculty, staff, and students.

name: Tulane University School of Medicine
summary: One of the nation's most recognized centers for medical education, Tulane University School of Medicine is a vibrant center for education, research and public service.

name: Tulane Primate Research Center
description:
Tulane National Primate Research Center (TNPRC) improves human and animal health through basic and applied biomedical research. As one of the seven National Primate Research Centers funded by the National Institute of Health, the TNPRC is committed to discovering causes, preventions, treatments, and cures that allow people around the world to live longer, healthier lives. Primary research interests include developing vaccines, treatments and diagnostic tools for infectious diseases such as AIDS, Lyme disease, malaria and tuberculosis.

Research at the Tulane National Primate Research Center is focused on understanding human health problems, the majority of which are infectious diseases that require the use of nonhuman primates to develop diagnostics, therapeutics and preventive strategies such as vaccines.

Originally opened in 1964 as the Delta Regional Primate Center, the Center is situated on 500 acres of land in Covington, Louisiana approximately forty miles north of New Orleans. Since that time, the Center has continually expanded its facilities, increased faculty and staff, and experienced steady growth in its research programs. It has become a premier infectious disease research facility and one of the largest employers in St. Tammany Parish with over three hundred employees and an economic impact estimated at $70.1 million a year.
summary: Tulane National Primate Research Center (TNPRC) improves human and animal health through basic and applied biomedical research. The TNPRC is committed to discovering causes, preventions, treatments, and cures.

name: Cognitive Studies
description: Cognitive Studies is an interdisciplinary coordinate major in the field of Cognitive Science.  In addition to exciting classes, we also host events.  Stay updated on upcoming events through Wavesync!
summary: Cognitive Studies is an interdisciplinary coordinate major based in the Philosophy Department.

name: Tulane Institute on Water Law & Policy
summary: We work to foster understanding of the complex relationship between people and water. We provide law and policy analysis to decision makers and the public to ensure that water and the ecosystems it supports are maintained for future generations.

name: French and Italian Department
description: The Department of French and Italian at Tulane University offers a comprehensive program of study that reflects our commitment to cross-cultural and interdisciplinary approaches. Our Department builds on the historical identity of New Orleans as a center of Francophone and Caribbean culture and as the hub of one of America's oldest Italian communities.
summary: The French and Italian Department is designed to provide students with the necessary skills to communicate effectively in French and Italian both orally and in writing.

name: Department of Comparative Literature
description:
The Comparative Literature department is a place where rigorous, multilingual study meets innovative theory and close reading. Our faculty’s expertise spans diverse literary traditions, fostering a unique blend of historical insight and critical analysis in literature, film, and media.

Besides offering courses in Comparative Literature, the department includes programs in German and Slavic Studies. As part of our language programs, students can take classes in German, Russian, and other Slavic languages, and have the opportunity to study abroad and perfect their language skills.
summary: The Comparative Literature department is a place where rigorous, multilingual study meets innovative theory and close reading.

name: Department of Environmental Health Sciences
description:
The Department of Environmental Health Sciences (EHS) engages in multi-faceted research and prepares culturally competent professionals to address all aspects of our environment: physical, chemical, biological, social, and policy. The health of communities is inextricably linked to the environment and pollutants disproportionally impact minority populations. Safe air, water, soil, and food are prerequisites for health. EHS graduates are leaders in public, private, not-for-profit and academic sectors globally.

The EHS transdisciplinary research portfolio spans basic toxicology to clinical biomarker science and community-engaged environmental health studies. We place priority on countering environmental health disparities locally, in the Gulf Coast region, across our nation, and around the world. EHS faculty have expertise in environmental health, toxicology, water quality, industrial hygiene, environmental health policy, community-based studies and resiliency.
summary: The Department of Environmental Health Sciences conducts research and educates culturally competent scientists and leaders to address the impact of the environment on the health of populations through scholarly research and hands-on practice experiences.

name: Africana Studies Program
description:
The Africana Studies Program at Tulane embraces interdisciplinary methodologies for the study of Africa and its diasporas across the globe, as well as for the critical study of race and anti-Black racism. The Program supports faculty and students who study Black experiences through teaching, research, scholarship, artistic expression, academic initiatives, public programming, and community engagement. As the one academic unit on campus that centers Blackness and the critical study of race, our work amplifies the significance of Black history, the beauty of Black creativity, the power of Black resistance, and a commitment to Black freedom. It also fosters the critical conversations about systemic and structural racism necessary for cultivating an anti-racist campus community.

 

Looking to declare your major or minor in Africana Studies?

Please email africana@tulane.edu to get the declaration process started!
summary: The Africana Studies Program at Tulane embraces interdisciplinary methodologies for the study of Africa and its diasporas across the globe, as well as for the critical study of race and anti-Black racism.

summary: Department of Sociology

name: Tulane ByWater Institute
description: The ByWater Institute is a nationally recognized environmental research institute focused on solutions science that addresses the grand challenges facing the ecosystems, communities and businesses along coastlines and rivers of the US. Using the Mississippi River Basin as our testbed, ByWater's growing number of experts and partners will advance a new scholarly approach to solutions science called “clinical trials for planetary health” — diagnosing problems and producing, testing and communicating solutions at unprecedented speeds and scales. ByWater’s overriding goal: Help create cities, coastlines and river basins in the region and beyond that are Thriving ByWater in an era of increasing climate extremes.
summary: Our mission is to catalyze thriving climate and water futures through transdisciplinary & collaborative scholarship

name: Department of Biostatistics and Data Science
description:
The Department of Biostatistics and Data Science advances biostatistics, bioinformatics and data sciences by conducting original methodological research, collaborating on interdisciplinary research teams, training students in the application of biostatistics and bioinformatics methods and public health data analytics, and providing high quality services to the academic, research and professional communities.

The BIOS faculty take great pride in providing a strong nurturing learning environment and are very accessible to students. Faculty are highly engaged in collaborative and independent research and encourage student participation in research projects both within and outside the department.
summary: The Department of Biostatistics and Data Science is a part of Tulane University School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine (TU SPHTM). The department advances biostatistics, bioinformatics and data sciences.

name: Department of Social, Behavioral, and Population Sciences
description:
The Department of Social, Behavioral, and Population Sciences (SBPS) prepares future generations of highly skilled, socially conscious, and culturally sensitive public health professionals for careers in program development, research, evaluation, and community–based health. The department’s central goal will be the advancement of health equity. A broad mix of public health social scientists working in New Orleans and around the world provide our diverse students with a rigorous grounding in social, behavioral, and population science methods as applied to health interventions and etiologic research at the individual, community, and societal levels. This work sheds light on the behavioral, social, and structural underpinnings of health inequities, and potential solutions to address them. Through coursework, discussions, and related activities, SBPS faculty guide students in their understanding of health as it relates to decision-making; economic, social, and cultural stratification; and power, place, and history.

Department faculty include scholars committed to interdisciplinary research, teaching, and practice and represent a variety of disciplines including psychology, sociology, social epidemiology, anthropology, economics, demography, and urban planning. Faculty research interests and possible content areas students will be able to pursue include:

 

  • Social determinants of health and health equity –– including social policy, racism, neighborhood conditions, and violence
  • Social epidemiology –– including qualitative and quantitative methods such as geographic information systems and data science
  • Health promotion and prevention science –– including m-health and multilevel intervention development as well as social innovation
  • Public health demography –– including migration, displacement, and mobility
  • Community engagement and participatory research –– including asset-based, strengths–based, and human–centered design

Cross-cutting areas in the department and throughout the school include research and training in nutrition, maternal and child health, and sexual and reproductive health, all of which are vitally important to understanding and promotion of health equity.
summary: Department of Social, Behavioral, and Population Sciences

name: Tulane Law School Diversity and Student Life
description: Tulane Law School's Diversity and Student Life department manages, assists, and creates programming for the law school diversity initiatives and student organizations.
summary: Tulane Law School's Diversity and Student Life department manages, assists, and creates programming for the law school diversity initiatives and student organizations.

name: Poker Power Tulane
description:
Poker Power Tulane is dedicated to teaching women and gender non-conforming students at Tulane how to play Texas Hold ’Em, working to reduce the underrepresentation of these groups in poker. Poker cultivates valuable skills such as capital allocation, risk management, and overall confidence.

Participation in Poker Power Tulane’s programming is open to all eligible enrolled students, regardless of skill level or gender identification.

Membership and participation in this organization are open to all students, regardless of their demographic identity.
summary:
This semester’s lessons are held Mondays at 6 PM in Howard-Tilton Memorial Library, Room B17.

Poker Power is a national organization committed to teaching women, gender non-conforming individuals, and their allies how to play Texas Hold'em poker.

Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

name: NTC Office of Undergraduate Research and Fellowships
description:
The NTC Office of Undergraduate Research and Fellowships (URAF) is the hub for information on research and grant funding opportunities, and nationally competitive merit-based scholarships and fellowships.

We take inspiration to serve our community from Latin words long associated with Tulane — Docere, Illuminare, Ducere, and updated them to meet our context within NTC:

— Inform, Empower, Accompany.

  • We serve to inform the Tulane community that research, grant, and scholarship/fellowship opportunities exist for every demographic, and everyone is welcome to engage with us to explore pathways to enhance and reach their goals.
     
  • We empower students who undertake the application process to use it to self-reflect, hone communication skills, and build conduits to their future selves, regardless of competition outcomes.
     
  • URAF provides students with growth-focused direction and assistance. We accompany and build rapport to better envision new outcomes and to open paths for reaching them.
summary: The NTC Office of Undergraduate Research and Fellowships (URAF) is the hub for information on research and grant funding opportunities, and nationally competitive merit-based scholarships and fellowships.

name: Law School - Online Legal Education
summary: Tulane's Online Legal Education Programs, consisting of the MJ-LEL (Master's of Jurisprudence in Labor and Employment Law), MJ-E (Master's of Energy Law, and Master's of Environmental Law) and the Title IX Certificate Program.

description: The Department of English teaches undergraduate and graduate students in the field of English.

name: Global Guides
description:
Global Guides is a group of student leaders who are passionate about international education and helping other students maximize their global experiences. Our goals are to create meaningful connections among Tulane students, to promote international programs, and to create a vibrant, international community at Tulane University.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine Office of Research Development and Administration
description:
The Office of Research Development and Administration (ORDA) advances the research mission of the Tulane University School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine by providing overall strategic leadership for the research program. Broad areas of purview are research infrastructure and supporting research productivity.

The priorities of the Office of Research Development include:

  • Support SPHTM researchers in identifying and expanding internal and external sources of research funding
  • Increase success in attaining competitive funding awards
  • Create and administer internal research funding initiatives
  • Coordinate administrative support for proposal preparation and grant stewardship
  • Foster collaborative and interdepartmental research projects
summary: The Office of Research Development (ORDA) advances the research mission of the Tulane University School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine by providing overall strategic leadership for the research program.

name: Medicine - Biomedical Informatics & Genomics
description: The mission of the Center for Biomedical Informatics and Genomics (CBG) is to promote research and education in biomedical informatics, computational biology, various omics, drug repurposing, and translational research of human complex diseases/traits with the goal of accelerating the pace of scientific discovery, reducing mortality and morbidity, and improving the quality of human life.
summary: Center for Biomedical Informatics and Genomics (CBG)

name: Tulane University Leadership Institute
description: As part of President Fitts' Plan for Now, the Tulane University Leadership Institute (TULI) supports the University’s focus on creating a diverse network of leaders in all areas of the University. The TULI promotes a dynamic culture of learning by providing employees personal and professional growth opportunities focused on levels of leadership, meaningful exploration and discovery, collaborative engagement, and a commitment to excellence.
summary: Provides professional development workshop and opportunities for Tulane's Faculty and Staff

name: The New Orleans Book Festival at Tulane University
description: The fourth annual New Orleans Book Festival at Tulane University will be held March 27-29, 2025 on Tulane University's uptown campus and is free and open to the public. Seating is available on a first-come, first-served basis.
summary: The New Orleans Book Festival will bring the world’s leading authors to Tulane University for a multi-day celebration of their works.

name: Tulane University Center for Sport
description:
The Tulane Center for Sport is a collaborative hub for all aspects of the sports ecosystem. Backed by Tulane University and relationships spanning local, national, and international sports organizations at all levels, the Tulane Center for Sport offers an interdisciplinary network of resources, knowledge, and experience that provides us the unique position to view sport from a 360-degree perspective.

Our work promotes cross-campus connections that allow us to examine crucial problems facing athletes, sport and society, as well as develop careers and innovative answers to issues that affect the sports industry. University professionals from every academic and practice area at Tulane engage their expertise and collaborate to advance our mission.
summary: The Center for Sport is devoted to studying, supporting, and achieving in all areas where sport engages people: the field of play, human health, professional careers, and in our community. Through sport, we educate, unite, challenge, inspire and i

Members of TREG wish to build a strong and productive network of connections in the real estate industry that supports and encourages the members to pursue their goals. TREG will offer opportunities for members to learn about commercial real estate through site tours, office visits, case competitions, and networking events.

TASA aims create an atmosphere where students and those surrounding the Tulane community have an opportunity to gain a global perspective and appreciation for African culture. TASA also aims to provide a ‘home away from home’ for African students, attending the university, through a variety of programs.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

name: Department of Political Science
description: The Department of Political Science at Tulane University, the site of the founding of the American Political Science Association in 1903, is one of the oldest departments of its kind in the nation. Its mission is to contribute to a deeper understanding of politics, government, international affairs, and international development through faculty research and publication; to serve as a source of expertise to the public; and to educate students about the principal questions and debates in the discipline. The department is committed to teaching undergraduate and graduate students to analyze politics from a variety of perspectives, identify key concepts, research effectively, and communicate persuasively. Through service learning and internship programs, we provide students with the opportunity to be active and informed participants in politics. The department also engages in inter- and multidisciplinary teaching and research, and its faculty members are affiliated with a variety of related undergraduate and graduate programs. Our program in Political Science is dedicated to addressing the political processes, institutions, and phenomena that constitute political systems and political development—locally, nationally, and globally.
summary: Our mission is to contribute to a deeper understanding of politics, government, international affairs and development through faculty research and publication and to educate students about the principal questions and debates in the discipline.

name: Crochet Club
description:
Come make some creative friends who enjoy yarn and its possibilities! <3 No crochet experience needed. We also supply yarn and hooks to those who are just beginning their crochet journey.

View our Instagram @tulanecrochetclub for updates on meetings, events, and locations. You will also find the link to our GroupMe here, which you can request to join! We also send out meeting notifications here.

description:

name: Green Wave Sound
description:
Green Wave Sound (GWS) was established to create an easy way for student musical artists to take advantage of being in New Orleans and find gigs at venues throughout the city. Additionally, we have evolved to become a site for networking and professional development within the music industry. Students will have the opportunity to hear from guest lecturers and learn more about what it means to work with music. New Orleans is one of the greatest cities for music, so we’re trying to take advantage as much as we can! You don’t have to be an artist to join GWS. This is something that we are passionate about and believe it is a great opportunity for Tulane students to get performance exposure and music industry knowledge.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Green Wave Sound is a music business association that allows for students to connect and network with professionals across all facets of the music industry.

name: Center for K-12 STEM Education
description: The Center for K-12 STEM Education at Tulane University is preparing the next generation of entrepreneurs, engineers and scientists for success. Since 2007, this program has helped inspire over 13,000 middle and high school students and teachers.
summary: The Center for K-12 STEM Education is a part of the School of Science and Engineering Dean's Office. We plan events and programs throughout the year to increase interest in STEM subjects among K-12 students in the Greater New Orleans area.

name: Middle American Research Institute
description:
Founded in 1924, the Middle American Research Institute (M.A.R.I.) at Tulane University strives to promote greater understanding of the vibrant and diverse cultures of Middle America. It stewards an extensive collection of textiles, artifacts, and an archive of letters, field notes, maps, and photographs from the scores of field projects it has sponsored and continues to sponsor. We strive to make these accessible to researchers and the general public through a variety of exhibitions, workshops, and symposia.

Furthermore, M.A.R.I. continues to support anthropological, archaeological, ethnohistorical, linguistic, and ethnographic research projects throughout Mexico and Central America.
summary: Since 1924, the Middle American Research Institute at Tulane University has supported archaeological research, curated collections of artifacts and textiles and published scholarship on the ancient cultures of Mexico and Central America.

description:
The Formula SAE® series competitions challenge teams of university undergraduate and graduate students to conceive, design, fabricate, develop, and compete with small, formula-style vehicles. The competitions give teams the chance to demonstrate and prove both their creativity and engineering skills in comparison to teams from other universities around the world. Members from all backgrounds are welcome, especially those interested in design engineering, and business.

Formula SAE Highlights

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.

Tulane Formula Racing is home to the car community at Tulane 

Club events include F1 watch parties, Car Shows, Formula SAE, College Racing Series, and all forms of racing.

name: MeeT U Kpop Dance Crew
description:
MeeT U was established to bring people together through K-pop songs and dance, providing a place for all with similar interests to share their thoughts and learn to dance together.

 

We hold weekly dance lessons on Sundays at the Uptown campus, and have cover groups who learn full choreographies throughout the semester. We also host events throughout the semester, including a collaborative showcase at the end of both Fall and Spring semesters!

 

We always look forward to connecting with fellow community members interested in dancing, Kpop, and having fun! We're an open and inclusive club who welcome all, regardless of your demographic or identity, so check us out if you're interested in learning more about us!
summary:
Nice to Meet U!

We are MeeT U, a Kpop club with the purpose of bringing people together through Kpop and dance!

name: Strategy, Leadership and Analytics Minor
description: As an interdisciplinary minor, and through a curriculum divided into three components, it incorporates basic economic planning courses with classes throughout the arts, humanities, and social sciences as well as specially designed SLAM courses focusing on leadership, ethics, law, public relations, marketing, and strategy.
summary: The Strategy, Leadership, and Analytics Minor is an interdisciplinary program that prepares students to be leaders who can manage the dynamics of rapidly changing marketplaces and adapt strategically and creatively for future careers.

name: School of Liberal Arts- Communication
description: With over 150 majors, our faculty members offer a range of theoretical, historical, and praxis-oriented courses. We contribute to the Cinema Studies major and minor, as well as to the Digital Media Practices coordinate major. Our faculty and department also maintain partnerships with African & African Diaspora Studies, Asian Studies, Latin American Studies, and Gender and Sexuality Studies. As part of our commitment to New Orleans and the University’s undergraduate public service graduation requirement, we offer several service learning courses in video production, media literacy, and cultural production for local nonprofit agencies, educational institutions and businesses.
summary: With over 150 majors, our faculty members offer a range of theoretical, historical, and praxis-oriented courses.

name: The Department of Classical Studies
description: We offer major and minor tracks in Classical Studies, Latin, or Greek, and encourage all students to sample broadly among our offerings even while concentrating in a particular field. In addition to courses taught in the ancient languages, we teach literature in translation; history, art, and archaeology surveys and seminars; and courses on topics as varied as mythology and religion, Roman law, ancient leadership, and gender in the ancient world. Our program focuses on developing skills in critical thinking, reading, and writing, and all majors complete independent research projects. Our students find that exploring the past invites new perspectives on the modern world while opening doors to the future, and recent graduates have pursued advanced studies at the M.A. and Ph.D. level, medical school, law school, the Peace Corps, and careers ranging from teaching, to publishing, to business.
summary: Classical Studies focuses on understanding the world of the ancient Mediterranean—including Greece, Rome, and surrounding cultures—by studying its languages, history, art, and archaeology.

name: Experiential Learning-Law School
description: At Tulane Law, experiential courses and programs help students integrate theory and practice locally, nationally, and around the globe. Through clinics, externships, boot camps, labs, and pro bono opportunities, students build skills, gain mentors, choose a career path, and cultivate a lifelong commitment to public service.
summary: Tulane University Law School Office of Experiential Learning and Public Interest Programs

name: Center for Artificial Intelligence
summary: The Tulane Center of Excellence in Artificial Intelligence supports research and education in artificial intelligence, machine learning, and data science, with a focus on applications that pave the way toward a healthier, more connected global community.

name: NTC Honor Board
description: The Newcomb-Tulane College Honor Board is the departmental group of students and faculty who are responsible for resolving reports of academic misconduct for students in NTC. The Honor Board accepts new members in April and December.  Members receive training annually and continuing education monthly on topics related to hearing and adjudicating cases of cheating and plagiarism. The Office of Academic Integrity provides the administrative support for the Honor Board and also hosts educational sessions like Know the Academic Code week each semester. Applications may be submitted any time using this link.
summary: The Newcomb-Tulane College Honor Board is the departmental group of students and faculty who are responsible for resolving reports of academic misconduct for students in NTC.

name: MD/MPH Program
description: Tulane's MD/MPH combined degree program offers Tulane School of Medicine students a unique opportunity to build on their patient-based medical education with a population-based public health degree. The combined degree program is designed to be completed in four or five years, integrating the requirements for the School of Medicine with those from the School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine.
summary: Tulane University's MD/MPH Combined Degree Program

summary: The Office of Research Proposal Development assists faculty in the preparation and submission of complex grant proposals, particularly those for large, multi-investigator programs or research centers.

summary: Promotes innovation in health equity by facilitating active collaboration between diverse people and organizations to inform and harmonize research to advance actionable and sustainable solutions.

summary: The history department’s mission for our majors is to ground students in the foundations of the human experience. . In so doing, our mission helps fulfill Tulane University’s mission to enrich the capacity of its students to think, learn, and lead.

name: Biomedical Engineering
description: The Tulane Department of Biomedical Engineering has been a global leader in biomedical engineering scholarship since 1977. Our mission is to inspire and work with students as we develop and apply engineering methods to confront health science challenges. We strive for excellence in undergraduate and graduate education, meaningful and innovative research, and service dedicated to advancing the field of Biomedical Engineering.
summary: Department of Biomedical Engineering

name: Tulane Center for Clinical Research
description:
The Tulane Center for Clinical Research provides a broad range of services to offer clinical trial coordination and assist in the initiation and conduct of research. The mission of the Tulane Center for Clinical Research, translation of research from bench science to community health, provides insight into our perspective of the relationships that exist between all internal and external determinants and actual health outcomes!

 

Our goal is to reduce the time required to facilitate the journey of science from the laboratory to community health centers!

Learn more here: https://medicine.tulane.edu/clinical-translation-unit
summary: The Tulane Center for Clinical Research helps researchers conduct research studies.

name: Waveform Producers Club
description:
The Waveform Producers Club is the collaborative hub for music producers at Tulane. From EDM to Rap to Pop music, this is the place to refine your skills, connect with others, and showcase your sound.

What You'll Get:

  • Community: Join, collaborate, and grow with a network of producers working together to build a powerful music scene on campus. Connect with local singers and rappers to compliment your tracks.

  • Feedback: Learn directly from guest speakers (Billboard-charting producers, engineers, and industry veterans) who offer live mix critiques and insight into professional music production.

  • Opportunities: Share your tracks with music professionals and submit them for inclusion in Tulane’s Music Science and Technology semester concerts.

  • Beat Battles: Compete with fellow club members in monthly challenges that build your confidence for a chance to win a cash prize.

  • Resources: Tap into a shared Google Drive loaded with samples, drum kits, mixing templates, and more.

If you’re serious about making music and want to be surrounded with like-minded individuals, join The Waveform Producers to take your tracks to the next level.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: A club to help producers collaborate, grow, and learn how to produce and market their beats. A shared space for creatives to hone their craft and attain valuable skill to propel them forward.

name: Students Against Food Insecurity
description:
Students Against Food Insecurity's mission is to engage community service-oriented students in conversations and activities focusing on combatting the prevalence of food insecurity both on Tulane’s Campus and in the greater New Orleans area. The primary goals of the organization are focused on three main aspects: campus-wide education about food insecurity, our community pantry initiative, and food recoveries via TUFRN. As part of our campus education efforts, we hold informational meetings, discussions & events surrounding food insecurity, and uphold relationships with local organizations in order to learn about and from the communities most impacted by food insecurity. The community pantry is another initiative of the organization. Housed next door to the Chapel of the Holy Saints at 7215 Zimpel Street (cat-cornered to the boot) the pantry prioritizes assisting members of the Tulane community who silently suffer from food insecurity. While the Commons and its programs help with this issue, every student is entitled to have consistent food options and groceries in their rooms. For information on volunteer opportunities with SAFI and/or pantry updates, follow us on Instagram and reach out to the executive board members listed below.


Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Students Against Food Insecurity's mission is to engage community service-oriented students in conversations and activities focusing on combatting the prevalence of food insecurity both on Tulane’s Campus and in the greater New Orleans area.

name: Tulane Dietetic Internship
description:
The Tulane Dietetic Internship Program is a graduate-level supervised practice program for students who would like to become Registered Dietitians. It is a full-time non-degree program with a community emphasis. The dietetic internship at Tulane University School of Public Health is currently granted accreditation by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND) of the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics, 120 South Riverside Drive, Suite 2190, Chicago, IL 60606. 800/877-1600, ext 5400. Their website is https://www.eatrightpro.org/acend. 

The purpose of the Tulane Dietetic Internship is to provide a comprehensive and intensive supervised practice experience, with the opportunities to acquire the competencies necessary to function as an entry-level practitioner and to make the student registration eligible (to be qualified to register for the Registered Dietitian [RD] Exam).
summary: The Tulane Dietetic Internship Program is a graduate-level supervised practice program for students who would like to become Registered Dietitians. It is a full-time non-degree program with a community emphasis.

name: School of Medicine, Section of Nephrology
