Fostering collaboration between health professionals, students, and communities.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The purpose of DGH is to promote health, education, art, and other human rights among medical students and health professionals.

name: ENT Interest Group (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The Tulane ENT Interest Group exists to expose medical students to the field of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery (ENT) and to support their professional development within the specialty. The group fosters connections between students and Tulane’s ENT faculty, encourages participation in ENT-related lectures and educational events, and hosts activities designed to highlight the breadth of the field. In addition, the organization helps students access local shadowing and research opportunities, creates a community for ENT-interested students to connect with one another, and provides guidance on preparing a competitive residency application. Through these efforts, the Tulane ENT Interest Group aims to cultivate early interest in ENT, strengthen student engagement, and support future leaders in the specialty.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Tulane ENT Interest Group introduces medical students to Otolaryngology by providing opportunities for mentorship, education, and hands-on experiences.

name: Family Medicine Interest Group (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The purpose of FMIG is to provide students interested in Family Medicine with opportunities to learn about the field and connect with faculty and professionals. The Family Medicine Interest Group is dedicated to upholding Tulane University School of Medicine policies. The Tulane University School of Medicine FMIG Chapter has been created to offer a platform for medical students interested in family medicine, providing them with structured opportunities to connect and pursue their research goals. More specifically, FMIG will allow members to: 1. Facilitate Networking: Connect students with family medicine professionals, including faculty, residents, and practicing physicians. 2. Encourage Research: Support and promote research opportunities in family medicine and translational research initiatives. 3. Organize Events: Host workshops, seminars, and guest speaker events to discuss current topics in family medicine and primary care. 4. Foster Community Engagement: Create opportunities for students to participate in community health initiatives and outreach programs. 5. Enhance Skills: Provide training sessions on essential skills for family physicians, such as communication, patient-centered care, and clinical procedures. 6. Support Diversity: Promote inclusivity and diversity within the field of family medicine by encouraging underrepresented groups to pursue careers in this specialty. 7. Mentorship Opportunities: Establish mentorship programs that connect medical students with family medicine mentors for guidance and support.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The purpose of the Family Medicine Interest Group is to provide students interested in family medicine with opportunities to learn more about the field of family medicine.

name: Healthcare for Justice Impacted Communities (Graduate - MSG)
description:
HAJIC’s mission is to raise awareness of physicians’ considerations when caring for justice-impacted individuals (incarcerated people, formerly incarcerated people, people who are affected by the justice system, and their loved ones) and the healthcare experiences justice-impacted individuals face. We do this through speaker events, advocacy events, collaborations with community partners, and service initiatives. We hope to uplift the voices of justice-impacted individuals to bring forth needed change in healthcare systems.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: HAJIC raises awareness for healthcare issues faced by justice-impacted individuals through events, advocacy, and community partnerships, aiming to uplift their voices and drive change in healthcare systems.

name: Healthcare Management Student Leadership Association (Graduate - WSPH)
description:
Healthcare Management Student Leadership Association (HMSLA) HMSLA is a student-run organization that aims to cultivate leadership, professional development, and community engagement among students pursuing healthcare management. The association provides opportunities for networking with healthcare professionals, organizing educational workshops, hosting speaker events, and facilitating peer mentorship. HMSLA also coordinates internal meetings and initiatives to enhance members’ skills in healthcare administration, teamwork, and strategic planning, while fostering a collaborative and supportive student community.

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all (WSPHTM) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: A student-led organization dedicated to developing leadership skills, fostering professional growth, and connecting students interested in healthcare management through networking, events, and community initiatives.

name: History of Medicine Society (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The Tulane University History of Medicine Society is one of the oldest History of Medicine Societies in the United States, and the oldest student run organization of its kind. Founded in 1933 by a group of ten medical students led by B. Bernard Weinstein, the group has continued to promote and foster interests in the historical background of contemporary medicine and the tradition of the humanities in medicine. Activities HOMS is an active, inclusive organization that strives to involve all students at all levels, faculty, deans and the rest of the Tulane University Medical Community. HOMS sponsors several lunch lectures each semester, during which a guest lecturer shares an aspect of the history of medicine with students over lunch. Popular past lecture topics include a two part lecture on the History and Development of Western Medicine, and single lectures covering Yellow Fever, The Plague, the History of Tulane Medical, and the ever-entertaining (and not-medically related!) spring lecture on the History of Mardi Gras. In addition to these educational lectures, HOMS hosts an annual wine and cheese gathering with faculty, deans and students in the fall and the B. Bernard Weinstein Memorial Lecture and Banquet each spring. At the banquet, the five winners of the HOMS annual History of Medicine Paper Contest are announced and recognized formally for their achievement.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Tulane University History of Medicine Society promotes and fosters interests in the historical background of contemporary medicine and the tradition of the humanities in medicine.

name: Integrative Public Health Initiative (Graduate - MSG)
description:
An interdisciplinary public health effort between the Medical School, School of Public Health, School of Social Work, and Community partners. Community Resource Fair - We hope to have a yearly tabling event, where we invite local community organizations we could put in touch with patients to address a variety of public health needs, from housing and food to legal aid. A list of the organizations who came last year will be provided, along with their contact info (See below). Dr. Slipman was a great supporter of the event and can be a resource to expand the event in the future to hopefully be more open - possibly even to the public. II. Journal Club - To provide the opportunity for students to present on public health topics. Priority will be given to ongoing research projects, but in their absence recent publications in the field could be topics of presentation. III. MD/MPH Buddy System - T4 MD/MPH Students would be paired with an incoming student to serve as an advisor and answer questions, offer support, or even help with tutoring. Rachael Ruiz was assessing T4 interest. IV. Speaker series - The goal for the speaker series was to incorporate speakers from outside of the school of medicine to both the other Tulane disciplines and also community representatives.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: An interdisciplinary public health effort between the Medical School, School of Public Health, School of Social Work, and Community partners.

name: Interest Group in Oncology (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The Interest Group in Oncology (IGO) at Tulane University School of Medicine provides medical students with opportunities to explore the rapidly evolving field of oncology while engaging with the local medical and patient community. We invite practicing oncologists and other specialists to speak with students about their professional experiences, offering perspective on career paths within oncology and highlighting the diverse ways physicians contribute to cancer care, from clinical practice to research to advocacy. Additionally, IGO works closely with the Tulane Cancer Center to advance patient education initiatives, raise awareness about cancer prevention, and promote the importance of early screening and detection. By partnering on community-focused events, we strive to bridge the gap between medical education and public health impact. Lastly, we host monthly journal clubs where students can present cutting-edge oncologic research in a collaborative environment and gain insight into emerging directions in cancer care. These sessions also connect students with Tulane faculty and researchers who can serve as mentors and provide opportunities for scholarly projects. For medical students interested in oncology as a career, passionate about advancing cancer research, or eager to engage in community outreach, IGO offers opportunities to learn, connect, and make a difference.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Interest Group in Oncology (IGO) at Tulane University School of Medicine connects students with the field of cancer care through monthly journal clubs, faculty mentorship, and talks with local oncologists.

name: Jewish Medical Student Organization (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The Jewish Medical Student Organization (JMSO) at Tulane University School of Medicine is dedicated to fostering community, culture, faith, learning, and service among Jewish medical students. We aim to provide a supportive, inclusive space where members can share traditions, explore Jewish identity within the context of medical training, and engage in both spiritual and cultural enrichment. Having a group like JMSO helps students maintain a connection with a cultural and religious identity, build peer support networks, find balance, and engage with values such as service, ethics, and compassion in a communal setting. We also provide space for dialogue — for grappling with how faith, medicine, and personal values intersect, and for offering support during challenging times.

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: JMSO is the Jewish Medical Students’ Organization. JMSO aims to create a community for Jewish medical students at Tulane School of Medicine.

name: Military Medical Student Association (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The Tulane Military Medical Student Association (MMSA) is a social network and support system for Tulane medical students who are currently, have been, or are thinking about being in the military. This site allows MMSA members to view documents that will be helpful throughout our four years at Tulane, see who else is in the club, track upcoming events, and more!

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all (MSG) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Tulane Military Medical Student Association (MMSA) is a social network and support system for Tulane medical students who are currently, have been, or are thinking about being in the military.

name: OB/GYN Interest Group (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The foremost purpose of OB/GYN IG is to provide students the opportunity to learn more about the field of Obstetrics and Gynecology, make connections with current Tulane OBGYN faculty, and provide a place for interested students to meet and share ideas within the Tulane University School of Medicine community.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The OBGYN IG connects students with information and opportunities related to Obstetrics and Gynecology.

name: Palliative Care Interest Group (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The purpose of Palliative Care Interest Group is to provide a platform for exploration of the field of palliative medicine and supportive care. Its mission is to spark interest in the specialty, deepen understanding of palliative care practices, and foster members’ professional development through educational events, mentorship opportunities, and collaboration with field experts.

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all (Medical School) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Palliative Care Interest Group seeks to foster interest and growth in the field of palliative medicine and supportive care.

name: Public Health Doctoral Students (Graduate - WSPH)
description:
Public Health Doctoral Students (PHDS) is a student organization that represents and supports doctoral students across all departments within the School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine. PHDS aims to enhance the doctoral experience by fostering interdisciplinary collaboration, building community, and promoting academic, professional, and career development. Through educational, social, and networking opportunities, PHDS seeks to inspire, challenge, and support doctoral students as they advance their training and professional goals.

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all (WSPHTM School) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Public Health Doctoral Students (PHDS) fosters collaboration, community, and professional development among doctoral students across all departments in the School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine.

name: Raising Education & Awareness in Community Health  Club (Graduate - MSG)
description:
Raising Education and Awareness in Community Health (REACH) is a student-run program out of Tulane University School of Medicine that started in May 2024. Our aim is to have medical students educate and empower youth about health topics so they can be well-informed and make the best decisions for their health and well-being. We partner with schools in New Orleans to deliver interactive presentations. Past topic presentations include cardiovascular health, mental health, stress management, exercise, nutrition, substance abuse, and sleep habits. We pair our presentations with Kahoot games, and distribute prizes to students. We hope to deliver interactive sessions where students feel empowered with knowledge of their health.

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all (Medical School) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Student-run program that works with New Orleans schools to deliver health presentations.

name: Society for Young Black Public Health Professionals (Graduate - WSPH)
description:
The Society of Young Black Public Health Professionals is as an association dedicated to raising awareness for pertinent health issues affecting minorities on both a global and community level. The Society maintains a professional network for all members, alumni, faculty, and Tulane undergraduate students to foster a positive enduring image of the Tulane community through local outreach projects, fundraising, attendance at national health conferences, volunteerism, and overall helping to better serve the community where we gain our education. The Society throughout the year works hard to utilize and develop the skills and talents of our members in order to help make a healthier tomorrow for all.

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all (WSPHTM School) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Society of Young Black Public Health Professionals cultivates a student body focused on highlighting, addressing and combatting the public health disparities and inequities across black and underserved communities across New Orleans.

name: South Asian Medical Student Association (Graduate - MSG)
description:
SAMSA’s mission is to unify medical students and promote South Asian health initiatives across the United States. The goals of SAMSA are: To support medical students in their education. To promote research on issues related to South Asian health and wellness. To encourage advocacy for culturally competent care and address racial healthcare disparities. To develop a mentorship network for medical students. To promote discussion of South Asian health and culture within the medical education curriculum. To create a network of medical students working together to improve South Asian health in the U.S.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: SAMSA’s mission is to unify medical students and promote South Asian health initiatives across the United States.

name: Student Interest Group in Neurology (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The purpose of Tulane SIGN is to promote and foster interest in the field of Neurology within the Tulane University School of Medicine community. We work to inform all students about neurology, regardless of their future specialty. Events will aim to: To make medical students aware of opportunities available in the field of Neurology. To further neurologic knowledge of medical students. To foster mentorship between students, residents, and practicing neurologists To provide opportunities for medical students to participate in activities related to the field of Neurology including discussions by Neurologists, patient presentations, seminars, journal clubs, learning materials and arranged shadowing of Neurologists in the workplace.

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all (Medical School) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The purpose of Tulane SIGN is to promote and foster interest in the field of Neurology within the Tulane University School of Medicine community

name: Students for Sexual Health and Reproductive Equity (Graduate - WSPH)
description:
The purpose of SSHARE is to advance inclusive, equitable sexual health education, resources and advocacy. While our core mission focuses on sexual health equity, we recognize that sexual health is interconnected with broader public health and social justice issues. This organization also engages in education, awareness, and action on related public health and social issues, including but not limited to: reproductive justice, mental health, LGBTQ+ rights, healthy relationships, bodily autonomy, and access to care. We want to create an affirming, and empowering space for all individuals, and to foster collaboration across our campus and community.

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all (WSPHTM School) students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The purpose of SSHARE is to advance inclusive, equitable sexual health education, resources and advocacy. While our core mission focuses on sexual health equity, we recognize that sexual health is interconnected with broader public health...

name: Texas Law Society (Graduate - SBA)
description:
A community of like-minded students interested in practicing law in Texas. We host a range of firm networking events and job panels, in addition to general meetings focused on academic preparation and social gatherings. No need to be from Texas to join!

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Professional networking organization for law students interested in practicing in Texas.

name: Tulane Emergency Medicine Interest Group (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The Tulane Emergency Medicine Interest Group (EMIG) is committed to engaging medical students in the fast-paced and impactful world of emergency medicine. Our mission is to provide meaningful opportunities for learning, mentorship, and community service. Through hands-on workshops, simulation sessions, and skills training, students gain exposure to critical procedures such as suturing, airway management, and point-of-care ultrasound. We also facilitate shadowing opportunities in the Emergency Department and connect students with residents and faculty mentors who guide their professional development. Beyond clinical exposure, EMIG is expanding its outreach this year with a stronger focus on CPR education, equipping both medical students and the community with lifesaving skills. Our group also encourages involvement in research, community service projects, and networking events, highlighting the vital role of emergency physicians in caring for diverse and vulnerable patient populations. Whether you are certain about pursuing EM or simply curious about the specialty, EMIG provides a welcoming space to explore, learn, and grow.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Tulane EMIG fosters interest in emergency medicine through hands-on workshops, shadowing, and mentorship. We offer skills training, research, and community outreach—expanding this year with CPR education for students and the public.

name: Tulane Med Climbing Club (Graduate - MSG)
description:
TMC aims to foster a community of students interested in indoor and outdoor rock climbing while promoting safety, inclusivity, and personal growth. In aspiration of these goals, we will host: climbing meetups at local gym; plan trips to other nearby climbing gyms or outdoor climbing areas; provide educational workshops on climbing techniques, equipment uses, and safety practices; host movie nights and other social events outside of climbing; facilitate mentorship between experienced climbers and beginners; organize at least one community service event annually to give back to the New Orleans outdoor/recreational community; promote health, wellness, and teamwork through climbing.

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The foremost purpose of TMC is to foster a community of students interested in indoor and outdoor rock climbing while promoting safety, inclusivity, and personal growth.

name: Tulane Real Estate Law Society (Graduate - SBA)
description:
The Tulane Real Estate Law Society is a student-run organization at Tulane Law School dedicated to supporting students interested in real estate and property-related legal careers. The Society hosts panels, speaker events, and networking opportunities with practicing attorneys, developers, and alumni, and provides professional development resources focused on real estate transactions, finance, land use, and development. It also fosters a collaborative community where students can explore the practical and academic dimensions of real estate law.

 

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: TRELS brings together students interested in real estate law and related fields, provides networking and professional development opportunities with practitioners, hosts educational events and panels on real estate topics, and helps members explore caree

name: Tulane Surgery Interest Group (Graduate - MSG)
description:
The Tulane Surgery Interest Group (TSIG) is a student-run organization at Tulane University School of Medicine dedicated to advancing knowledge of the field of surgery through education, experiential learning, and research. TSIG offers students numerous opportunities to immerse themselves in the surgical field, including a mentorship program, simulation center skills workshops, and an annual T4 Match Panel with graduating students. The group also hosts speaking events featuring surgeons and residents who share insights into the latest surgical advancements, residency experiences, and career pathways. Through its programs, panels, and hands-on opportunities, TSIG aims to equip Tulane medical students with the knowledge, mentorship, and practical experience to explore and pursue a career in surgery.

Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all Medical School students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: The Tulane Surgery Interest Group (TSIG) is a student-run organization that fosters surgical education through mentorship, skills workshops, speaker events, and research opportunities, helping students explore and engage with the field of surgery.

name: Tulane Women in Law Society (Graduate - SBA)
description:
Tulane Women in Law (TWIL) is an organization committed to examining and discussing issues affecting women in both substantive areas of the law and in the legal profession. To reach this objective, Law Women sponsors speakers, panel discussions, and workshops, which focus on the nexus of gender and the law. Tulane is an equal opportunity educator. Legally protected demographic classifications such as race, national origin, sex, age, disability, veteran status etc. are not relied upon as an eligibility or participation criteria for educational programs or activities. It is our mission to celebrate the plethora of opinions and ideas at our school.
 
Participation or membership in this organization is nondiscriminatory and open to all students regardless of demographic identity.
summary: Tulane Women in Law (TWIL) is an organization committed to examining and discussing issues affecting women in both substantive areas of the law and in the legal profession. To reach this objective, Law Women sponsors speakers, panel discussions, and...

name: Tulane Civic Engagement Coalition
summary: Civic Engagement involves “working to make a difference in the civic life of one’s community and developing the combination of knowledge, skills, values and motivation to make that difference. It means promoting the quality of life in a community.

organizationType.name: Center for Public Service Leadership program
